# Acts of the General Assembly
## of the
# Commonwealth of Kentucky



Passed at the Regular Session of the General Assembly, which was begun in the city of Frankfort, Kentucky, on Tuesday, January the seventh, 1964, and ended Friday, March the twentieth, 1964.

Frankfort, Kentucky
June 1, 1964

FOREWORD
The 1954 General Assembly imposed upon the Legislative
Research Commission the duty of furnishing the printer correct
copy of bills and resolutions for inclusion in the Kentucky Acts .
House Bill No. 440 of 1964 added the requirement that the
Legislative Research Commission prepare indexes to
alleditions
ofthe Kentucky Acts after proofreading from the enrolled bills
.
This volume represents the fulfillment
ofthese obligations
.
This edition
ofthe Kentucky Acts continues the practice
,
initiated
in1962
,
ofcarrying new material
initalics and deleted
material
inbrackets when
abill amends
anexisting section
of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes
. By this procedure
, members
of
the General Assembly and other interested persons may readily
determine changes
inexisting law without constant referral
to
the Kentucky Revised Statutes
.
Inthe preparation
ofthis volume
ofthe 1964 Kentucky Acts
the Secretary
ofState has furnished access
tocopy
offiled en-
rolled bills and resolutions
. The staff
ofthe Legislative Research
Commission compared the prepared page proofs with the enrolled
bills
. The staff
isespecially grateful
toSecretary
ofState Thelma
Stovall and Mrs. Lucille Barclay
ofher staff for their cooperation
inmaking the files
ofthe enrolled bills available despite the fact
that
itmust often have interferred with their office routine
. In
order
toexpedite furnishing copy
tothe printer
, bills and resolu-
tions are inserted by chapters according
tothe order
inwhich
they received gubernatorial action and were filed
inthe office
of
the Secretary
ofState
. Mr. Courtenay
J.Walker
, Administrative
Assistant
tothe Director
, has had primary responsibility for the
preparation
ofthis volume
.
Frankfort
, Kentucky
June
1, 1964
HARRY LEE WATERFIELD
Chairman
JAMES
T.FLEMING
Director

# INDEX

The index is a valuable tool for finding specific information in a book. It is usually located at the end of the book and lists the topics covered in the book along with the page numbers where they can be found.

The index is organized alphabetically by topic. This makes it easy to find the information you are looking for.

The index is a great way to review the material in a book. It can help you to remember the key points of the book.

The index is also a great way to find information on a specific topic. If you are looking for information on a specific topic, you can use the index to find the page numbers where that topic is discussed.

The index is a valuable tool for students, researchers, and anyone who wants to find information in a book.

## WATERFORD

The city of Waterford is located in the southeastern part of Ireland. It is the oldest city in Ireland and was founded by the Vikings in the 9th century.

Waterford is a popular tourist destination and is known for its beautiful architecture, history, and culture.

The city is home to a number of museums, galleries, and other cultural attractions.

Waterford is also a major port city and is an important center for trade and commerce.

The city is located on the River Suir and is connected to the rest of Ireland by a network of roads and railways.

Waterford is a vibrant and dynamic city with a rich history and a bright future.

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL ASSEM-
BLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT , KEN-
TUCKY, ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE SEVENTH , 1964 , AND ENDED
FRIDAY, MARCH THE TWENTIETH , 1964 .
CHAPTER 1
(S. B. 2)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Lookout Heights , in
Kenton County .
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Lookout Heights , in
Kenton County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of
the fifth class ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
The City of Lookout Heights , in Kenton County, is trans-
ferred from the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Signed January 29 , 1964
CHAPTER 2
(H. R. 6 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the Legislative
Research Commission for special expenses incurred in connection
with sessions of the General Assembly and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , additional personnel are necessary to expedite properly
the business of the Senate and the House of Representatives ; and

2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , the present appropriation of the Legislative Research Com-
mission is insufficient to provide adequate clerical services to the
General Assembly :
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
That there be appropriated out of the General Expenditure
Fund to the Legislative Research Commission the sum of one
thousand and seven hundred and fifty dollars per calendar day
during
allregular and special sessions
ofthe General Assembly
.
This appropriation shall
beavailable for expenditure during the
1963-1964 fiscal year
.
WHEREAS
, the General Assembly
isnow
insession and
it
is
necessary that these funds be available immediately
,an emer-
gency
isdeclared
toexist and this resolution shall become effec-
tive upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
, and shall
beretroactive
toinclude payment
ofcompensation
tosuch per-
sonnel from and including January
7, 1964
.
Signed January 29
, 1964

3
(H. R.
7)

aCommission
on Economy and Efficiency
.

, methods and procedures for the elimination of waste and

.

Be
itresolved by the General Assembly

ofKentucky
:

1. There
ishereby established
aCommission on
Economy and Efficiency
. The purpose
ofthe Commission on
Economy and Efficiency shall
be
toadvise the General Assembly

CHAPTER 8
(S. B. 4)
AN ACT relating to medical and dental education and research .
WHEREAS , the need for medical and dental education and research
in Kentucky is plain and clear ; and
WHEREAS , the University of Louisville School of Medicine is one of
only two medical colleges , and the University of Louisville School of
Dentistry is one of only two dental colleges in the Commonwealth ;
and
WHEREAS , the rising costs of medical and dental education and research
are such that the University of Louisville Schools of Medicine and
Dentistry cannot meet the expenses of their programs from the
resources presently available to them :
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) The General Assembly recognizes the con-
tinuing need for medical and dental education and research in
Kentucky , and that the need is larger than the capacity of the
facilities of the state university to supply . The General Assembly
finds that the University of Louisville Medical and Dental Schools
help to supply , for the entire state , this need existing in the fields
of medical and dental education and research , and declares that
support of the University of Louisville Medical and Dental
Schools is a public purpose for which public money may be
validly expended .
( 2 ) The University of Louisville shall keep
itsbooks and
records available
tothe State Auditor
. An audit
ofexpenditure

14 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of public money by the University is subject to the laws gen-
erally governing audits of expenditures of public money .
Section 2. Section 164.025 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is repealed .
Signed February 28 1964

municipal revenue bonds is favorable to the issuer of such bonds ,
and any delay in the enactment of enabling legislation may pre-
vent the Authority from taking advantage of the situation , an
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
.

CHAPTER 11

WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Saint Regis Park , in
Jefferson County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of
the fifth class ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
The City of Saint Regis Park in Jefferson County , is trans-
ferred from the sixth to the fifth class of cities .

wise :
Section 1. KRS 313.010 is amended to read:
As used in this chapter , unless the context requires other-
( 1 ) "Board " means the Kentucky State Board of Dental
Examiners .
( 2 ) Any person shall be regarded as "practicing dentistry "
who , for a fee, salary or other reward paid , or to be paid either to
himself , or to another person , performs or advertises to perform ,
dental operations of any kind, or who diagnoses or treats diseases
or lesions of human teeth or jaws , or attempts to correct mal-
positions thereof , or who diagnoses or treats disorders , or de-
ficiencies of the oral cavity and adjacent associated structures , or
who takes impressions of the human teeth or jaws , or shall con

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
struct, supply, reproduce or repair any prosthetic denture , bridge ,
artificial restoration , appliance or other structure to be used or
worn as a substitute for natural teeth , except upon the written
order or [ of ] prescription of a licensed dentist and constructed
upon or by the use of casts or models made from an impression
taken by a licensed dentist , or who shall advertise , offer , sell [, ]
or deliver any such substitute or the services rendered in the con-
struction , reproduction , supply or repair thereof to any person
other than a licensed dentist , or who places or adjusts such sub-
stitute in the oral cavity of another , or who uses the words "den-
tist, " "dental surgeon," the letters “ D.D.S. , ” “D.M.D. ,” or other
letters or title in connection with his name, which in any way
represents him as being engaged in the practice of dentistry .
( 3 ) "Dental hygiene " shall mean the treatment of human
teeth by scaling , polishing , planing and removing therefrom cal-
careous deposits , and by removing accumulated accretion from di-
rectly beneath the free margin of the gums; [, and by polishing
the exposed surface of the teeth , and shall also mean ] the making
of X-ray exposures of teeth and surrounding tissues ; the clinical
examination of the teeth and surrounding tissues for carious le-
sions , periodontal pockets and other abnormal conditions ; the ob-
taining and recording of basic medical and dental histories and
information ; the instruction of patients in proper tooth care; the
application of sodium or stannous fluoride or other substances
beneficial in the control of caries ; the application of desensitizing
agents ; the polishing , and smoothing of rough edges , of restora-
tions ; and the cementing of facings and pontics outside of a pa-
tient's mouth ; provided , that nothing in KRS 313.260 to 313.350
shall be so construed as to affect the practice of medicine or den-
tistry nor to prevent students of a dental college or university
from practicing dental hygiene under supervision of their instruc-
tors ; [ . ] and , provided further , that nothing in KRS 313.260 to
313.350 shall be construed to authorize any dental hygienist to
perform any operation in a patient's mouth without supervision
of a dentist within the dentist's office .
[ ( 4 ) "Dental Hygienist " shall mean any person who prac-
tices dental hygiene .]

stitute a separate offense .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth

Section 1. KRS 189.390 is amended to read:
( 1 ) No operator of a vehicle upon a highway shall drive
at a greater speed than is reasonable and prudent , having regard
for the traffic and for the condition and use of the highway .

for compliance with subsection ( 1 ) of this section, the speed
of any vehicle not in excess of the limits specified in this section
or established as hereinafter authorized shall be lawful , but
any speed in excess of the limits specified in this section or
established as hereinafter authorized shall be unlawful .
(a) For all vehicles other than motor trucks , semitrailer
trucks , or motor vehicles of five horsepower or less , thirty-five
miles per hour in any business or residential district, sixty miles
per hour in other locations during the daytime and fifty miles
per hour in other locations during the nighttime ;

per hour in any business or residential district , fifty miles per hour
in other locations .

miles per hour in any location .

shall have the meanings respectively ascribed to them:
(a) "Business district," the territory contiguous to and
including a highway when within any six hundred feet along such
highway there are buildings in use for business or industrial
purposes which occupy three hundred feet of frontage on one
side or three hundred feet collectively on both sides of the
highway ;
(b ) "Residential district ," the territory contiguous to and
including a highway not comprising a business district when
the property on such highway for a distance of three hundred
feet or more is in the main improved with residences or residences
and buildings in use for business ;
(c) " State highway ," a highway or street maintained by the
Department of Highways ;
(d) "Nighttime ," the period from one-half hour after sunset
until one-half hour before sunrise, and at such other periods as
atmospheric conditions render visibility as low as or lower than is

officials .

Section 7. The Division of Purchases shall establish a pro-
gram of inspection and quality control which shall include , among
other things :
( 1 ) Formal regulations regarding inspection and reporting
procedures to be followed by receiving agencies , including prompt
reporting to the Division of Purchases of all deviations from the
quantity and quality of goods specified in the contract under
which delivered ;
(2) Spot checks by the Division of Purchases of deliveries
to determine agency compliance and vendor performance ;
( 3 ) Verification of composition and performance of goods
to determine if they meet specifications of contract , for which
purpose the division may employ testing facilities and personnel
of state agencies or of federal agencies and independent testing
laboratories ;
(4 ) On-the -job training and other instruction of personnel
responsible for receiving goods at the various state institutions
and agencies .
Section 8. Sections 42.060 , 42.070 , and 45.280 , and sub-
section ( 5 ) of 42.990 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are re-
pealed .
Section 9. This Act becomes effective July 1, 1964 .

The State Board of Education shall not approve any working
budget or salary schedule for local boards of education for the
school years 1964-65 and 1965-66 in which the total amount
budgeted for teachers ' salaries is not equal to the amount budgeted

CHAPTER 17 55
for teachers' salaries in 1963-64 plus the additional amount for
teachers ' salaries and guarantee received by the district under
this appropriation . The amount which any district receives under
any guarantee provision shall be presumed to be for teachers '
salaries and shall be placed in the teachers' salaries budget and
allotted to the various teachers' salary ranks in a proportionate
manner in keeping with KRS 157.390 . No working budget or
salary schedule shall be approved in which the average salary
paid any rank of teacher is not at least equivalent to the allot-
ment for that rank and in which any teacher is paid less than
ninety per cent of the Foundation Program allotment for that
rank , except where there is a decline in local revenue of that
district after the district has levied the maximum ad valorem tax
rate permitted by law. Any such reduction permitted shall be
only for the amount of the decline in local revenue .
In the event any local board of education claims there is a
decline in local tax resources in that district , the State Board of
Education shall certify such claim to the Department of Revenue
whose duty it shall be to determine whether or not there is a
decline in local tax resources of such district and if so, the amount
of such decline in local tax resources . The Department of Revenue
shall make its certification to the State Board of Education and
said Board shall accept said certification as final .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot to
any school district more basic classroom units than the number
of teachers employed by that school district for basic classroom
units . Entitlement for basic classroom units obtained in schools
or buildings of one classification shall not be allocated to schools
or buildings of another classification , for which purposes the
classifications shall be as follows :
( 1) One -teacher schools ;
(2) Schools , other than one-teacher schools , with less than
one hundred pupils in average daily attendance; and ,
(3) Schools , other than one-teacher schools , with more than
one hundred pupils in average daily attendance .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall allot class-
room units for superintendents , principals , their certified assistants

subsection 6, only for personnel who are qualified for their re-

The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot a
full classroom unit for any non -isolated one-teacher school with
fewer than 27 pupils in average daily attendance and no full
classroom units shall be allotted for any one- teacher school which
has fewer than 12 pupils in average daily attendance .
Facilities that are used to house school children shall be
approved by the Superintendent of Public Instruction under KRS
162.060 and 162.160 . No alterations or additions of old buildings
shall be made without prior approval of the Superintendent of
Public Instruction . No space shall be rented or purchased and
converted to classroom use without prior approval of the Super-
intendent of Public Instruction . The State Board of Education
may adopt regulations for the approval on an annual basis of
temporary facilities for the housing of school children but these
regulations shall not permit the division of any present room by
partitions of nonstructural material . No classroom units shall be
allotted for a second teacher in the same classroom . No facilities
shall be approved for classroom use which are detrimental to the
health of or dangerous to the safety of school children .
If a school district operates any school or grade of a school
for a term of less than nine scholastic months or for a school day

III . ADMINISTRATION AND EFFECT
The Department of Highways shall submit an annual budget
for each fiscal year to the Department of Finance for approval by
the Commissioner of Finance immediately before the fiscal year
begins . The budget shall be based upon the most current state
road fund revenue estimates and contractual and noncontractual
federal aid for which the department will be eligible during the
fiscal year .
The appropriations made by this Act are subject to the
provisions of Chapters 12, 42, and 45 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes except where additional requirements are imposed by
this Act . Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year to the State Road Fund ending
June 30, 1964.
PART THREE
REVOLVING , TRUST AND AGENCY FUNDS
There is appropriated to the various boards , commissions ,
institutions , agencies and/or subdivisions of the State Govern-
ment only as are listed herein for the fiscal year ending June 30 ,
1965 , and for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1966 , all of the fees
(which include fees for board and room , athletics , and student
activities ) and rentals, admittances , sales , bond proceeds , licenses
collected by law, contributions , gifts , subventions , Federal grants
and other Federal receipts and other miscellaneous receipts pro-
duced or received by any of the following agencies of the State
Government , except as otherwise specifically provided , for the
use and benefit for each of the below - named agencies of the State
Government for the purposes for which each fund is authorized .
Receipts which are received by each of the respective agencies
of the State Government listed herein shall be placed to the credit
of a Revolving or Trust and Agency Fund out of which shall be

78 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
established separate Revolving or Trust and Agency Fund ac-
counts for the use and benefit of these agencies of the State Gov-
ernment for purposes for which each fund is authorized .
In the event receipts received and credited to any Revolving
or Trust and Agency Fund Account during fiscal year 1964-65 or
fiscal year 1965-66 and any balance forwarded to the credit of
any such account from the previous fiscal year exceed the ap-
propriation made by specific sum to said account in this Part of
this Appropriation Act , the excess is appropriated to the credit of
said account for the fiscal year in which the excess occurs ; pro-
vided , however , that said excess shall become available for ex-
penditure for the purpose of the account during said year only
with the authorization of the Commissioner of Finance and
approval of the Governor .
Except as herein otherwise provided , the appropriate agency
may requisition for expenditure from the Revolving or Trust
and Agency Fund Account , through the Department of Finance ,
during the fiscal year ending June 30, 1965 , and during the fiscal
year ending June 30, 1966 , all receipts placed to the credit of
the Revolving or Trust and Agency Fund for such year , out of
which has been established a Revolving or Trust and Agency
Fund account for each separate agency during such fiscal year ,
plus any balance which said agency may have had forwarded
from the preceding year on or before September 30, of the then
current fiscal year , by the Department of Finance . On or before
the beginning of each fiscal year , each department shall submit
to the Department of Finance schedules showing estimated re-
ceipts and estimated expenditures by quarterly period .
Notwithstanding any provisions of KRS 45.140 to the con-
trary , any balances remaining unallotted to specific projects of the
revolving funds authorized by Chapter 3, Acts of the General
Assembly , 1962 , to be established , upon approval of the Governor ,
to provide , respectively , loans for local airport development , and
loans for development of the arts and crafts industry , shall at the
end of the fiscal year 1963-64 lapse to the General Expenditure
Fund .

PART FOUR
GENERAL PROVISIONS
1. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any ap-
propriation made hereinbefore or hereinafter for the fiscal year
ending June 30, 1964 , and nothing in this Act is to be construed
as amending or altering the provisions of Chapter 42 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes pertaining to the duties and powers of
the Commissioner of Finance . All questions that arise in interpret-
ing any appropriation in this Act as to the purpose or manner
for which such appropriation may be expended shall be decided
by the Commissioner of Finance in accordance with the detailed
estimates embodied in the budget report , and supporting work
papers , as considered by the General Assembly , and the decision
of the Commissioner of Finance shall be final and conclusive .
2. The Commissioner of Finance is authorized to establish
a system and/or formula for pro-rating the administrative costs
of the Departments of Personnel , Finance , Treasury , and Law
relative to the administration of programs in which there is joint
participation by the State and Federal Government for the pur

90 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pose of receiving the maximum amount of Federal participation
permitted under the appropriate federal laws and regulations
governing such programs . Any reimbursements received from
Federal funds under this system or formula shall accrue to the
General Expenditure Fund and any system or formula for the
proration shall be developed pursuant to the provisions of KRS
18.330 .
3. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Act , the ap-
propriations to agencies receiving both a General Fund and a
Road Fund appropriation shall be considered one appropriation
for purposes of administration of this Act, and the rates of General
Fund -Road Fund appropriations may be adjusted , by the Com-
missioner of Finance with approval of the Commissioner of High-
ways , within the total authorized appropriation for any agency.
4. Notwithstanding any provisions of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes to the contrary , Eastern Kentucky State College , Western
Kentucky State College , Murray State College , Morehead State
College , Kentucky State College and the University of Kentucky
are hereby authorized and empowered , with the approval of the
Commissioner of Finance , to utilize funds available from any
appropriated source , for the purpose of cooperating to the fullest
extent possible with federal agencies in such programs as may be
proposed by the Federal Government involving the use of federal
funds for educational purposes, programs , and buildings in con-
junction with the colleges and the University .
5. In enacting this Appropriation Act , it is the deliberate
intention of the General Assembly to enact each section, and each
subsection thereof , as a separate and/ or specific appropriation and
law , and if any section , any subsection, or any provision thereof
shall be held invalid , or unconstitutional , the decision of the
courts shall not affect or impair any of the remaining sections ,
subsections , or provisions contained herein .
6. All continuing appropriations in existence at the time
this Act takes effect are discontinued and repealed by this Act ,
notwithstanding any provisions of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
to the contrary , except as provided by Chapters 12 , 42, 45, 56 ,
177 , and 341 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . All laws and
portions of laws in conflict with any of the provisions of the above
enactment , to the extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .

CHAPTER 17 91
7. Whereas , the finances of the Commonwealth cannot be
ascertained at this time , and since the actual needs of each of-
ficer , department , board , commission , institution , or subdivision
of the State Government can only be anticipated and not finally
determined by the General Assembly , and since the total amount
of revenue accruing to the General Expenditure Fund and to the
General Road Fund from which these appropriations are made
can only be anticipated through existing laws and those that may
hereafter be enacted , it is the purpose and intention of this act
that the expenditures for each fiscal year under Part I of this
Act shall not exceed the total financial resources anticipated to
accrue to the General Expenditure Fund and that the expendi-
tures for each fiscal year under Part 2 of this Act shall not exceed
the total financial resources anticipated to accrue to the General
Road Fund for the same fiscal year respectively . As the conditions
cannot be fully determined until after the adjournment of the
1964 General Assembly , and as the needs of each officer , depart-
ment , board , agency, commission , institution , or subdivision of
the State Government and the total financial resources accruing
to the General Expenditure Fund and General Road Fund cannot
be fully determined at this time , it is the intention and the pur-
pose of this General Assembly that the Commissioner of Finance ,
with the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and the approval
of the Governor of the Commonwealth , is authorized , empowered
and directed to ascertain and determine the needs of each officer ,
department , board , commission , institution or subdivision of the
State Government , and to ascertain the total financial resources
anticipated to accrue to the General Expenditure Fund and Gen-
eral Road Fund of the Commonwealth , and upon the ascretain-
ment and determination of said facts , he , the Commissioner of
Finance , in the manner set forth and as provided by general law
is authorized , empowered and directed by the General Assembly
to prevent an overdraft or deficit in any fiscal year for which
appropriations are herein made , by equitably reducing , according
to need , without discrimination , the appropriations herein made in
Part I and Part 2 to any officer , department , board , commission ,
institution , or subdivision of the State Government ; provided
that the power vested shall not permit any reduction of the ap-
propriation of any officer , department , board , commission , institu-
tion , or subdivision of the State Government that will actually

92 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
impair the necessary constitutional functions of any agency
whose operations and functions are determined to be necessary
constitutional functions of government .
Signed February 28 , 1964
CHAPTER 18
(Н. В. 76)
AN ACT relating to the management, maintenance , and operation of the
Executive Mansion and the Old Governor's Mansion , and declaring
an emergency .
WHEREAS , the Governor's Mansion and the Old Governor's Mansion
are owned , operated, and maintained by the Commonwealth ; and
WHEREAS , the Governor and Lieutenant Governor in their official
capacities are required to entertain extensively in behalf of the
Commonwealth , particularly in the interest of furthering the indus-
trial , tourist , and other economic development of the Commonwealth ;
and
WHEREAS , it is to the interest of the Commonwealth that these Man-
sions should be maintained and operated in a fashion which will
reflect favorably on the reputation of the Commonwealth for gra-
cious hospitality ; and
WHEREAS , such operation and maintenance of the Mansions should
be accomplished in an orderly and efficient manner ; and
WHEREAS , the Department of Finance has been delegated the respon-
sibility of managing , maintaining , and operating these Mansions :
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 42.035 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) The Department of Finance shall be responsible for the
management , maintenance , and operation , including the purchase

(2) A reasonable amount shall be deducted from the salary

(4) The Department of Finance shall maintain such records

of Kentucky :

of 4 1/3 ′ x 5 1/2' , and shall be affixed to a suitable staff and stand .

CHAPTER 22
(S. B. 57)
AN ACT relating to construction work which may be done by the Depart-
ment of highways :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Department of Highways may cause any road
construction or reconstruction to be done by employees of the
department instead of through contractors when , in the opinion
of the commissioner as expressed in an official order , any of the
following conditions is found to exist :
( a) Emergency construction or reconstruction is made
necessary by the fact that a road has been rendered unusable

100 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or the urgency of need for construction or reconstruction is
apparent .
(b) Construction or reconstruction of access roads would
serve as an inducement to industrial location or substantial ex-
pansion of industry , and the estimated cost of the project is less
than twenty -five thousand dollars .
(c) The project involves : an existing road and provides for
such work as improvement of sight distance , traffic marking ,
channelization or the addition or widening of traffic lanes or
widening of shoulders ; or construction , reconstruction , widening
or strengthening of small bridges and projects for which prep-
aration of detailed plans required to secure bids is unnecessary ;
or an emergency need does not allow for time for preparation of
plans and advertisement for bids .
(d) No satisfactory bid is received for the construction or
reconstruction of the road after all necessary steps have been
taken to obtain bids for such work, and the estimated cost of the
projects is less than twenty -five thousand dollars .
Section 2. Section 176.120 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is repealed .
Signed March 10, 1964

(4) No person shall stop or park a vehicle except when

CHAPTER 30
(Н. В. 48)
AN ACT relating to firemen in cities of the first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
A new Section of Chapter 95 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is created to read as follows :
Section 1. The fire department of each first class city shall
be divided into three platoons . Each platoon shall be on duty
for twenty -four consecutive hours , after which the platoon serving
twenty-four hours shall be allowed to remain off duty for forty-
eight consecutive hours , except in cases of emergency. The Chief
of the fire department shall arrange the schedule of working

110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
hours to comply with the provisions of this section . The pay ,
rank or benefits , of the members and officers of the fire depart-
ment , shall not be reduced as a result of this section .
Signed March 12, 1964

two persons , who shall be of different political parties , to serve
on and be members of the board of waterworks . Each appointee
shall be [ a city resident ] at least thirty years of age [, ] and shall
be a resident of the city , if appointed by the mayor , or of the
county , if appointed by the county judge , and be the owner in
his own right of real estate situated in the city if appointed by
the mayor , or in the county if appointed by the county judge .
At least one such appointee shall be qualified , as specified in
KRS 96.250 , to serve as president of the board . No officer or em-
ploye of the city, whether holding a paid or unpaid office , shall
be eligible for appointment to the board . Of the persons first
appointed one shall be appointed for a term of one year , one for a
term of two years , one for a term of three years , and one for a
term of four years and such terms shall expire on the date of the
annual meeting of the board of waterworks . Their successors
shall be appointed in the same manner, but for terms of four
years each . At such time as the board of waterworks serves on
a retail basis in excess of 50,000 customers outside the corporate
limits of a city of the first class , the president of the board shall
notify the county judge who shall immediately appoint two mem-
bers as hereinabove provided , one for a term of two years and one
for a term of four years ; and their successors shall be appointed
by the county judge for terms of five years each . Appointees
shall be eligible to succeed themselves . All vacancies shall be
filled for the unexpired term by appointment in the same manner .
Each member shall hold his office until his successor has been ap
pointed and qualified . The oath of office of each member shall be
filed with the board of sinking fund commissioners .

Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
As used in KRS 76.010 to 76.220 and in this act, unless the
context otherwise requires :
( 1 ) "District " means a metropolitan sewer district author-
ized by KRS 76.010 to 76.220 .
(2) "Board" means the board described in KRS 76.030 .
(3 ) "District area ,” “within the district ," "corporate limits
of the district " and similar terms mean that area of a county
containing a city of the first class which area was on April 1 ,
1964 , either inside the city of the first class or served by sanitary
or combined sanitary and storm water sewers which were under
the immediate control and custody of the district , that area of
such a county which is added to the district pursuant to KRS
76.170 , and that area of such a county which may be annexed
to the city of the first class except that no construction subdistrict
shall be construed to be within the district area .
(4) "Construction subdistrict bonds and obligations " and
like phrases mean any obligation whatsoever that has been in-
curred by the district because of some function or activity of
a construction subdistrict . Such debts are not obligations of the
district, and such debts may be paid only from monies received
by the district on account of the construction subdistrict , or from
the funds , if any , in the construction subdistrict reserve fund .

Section 12.
Anew section
ofthe Kentucky Revised Statutes
iscreated to read as follows
:
(1) The district may establish

when twenty
-five percent
ormore
ofthe freeholders

128 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
contiguous to other territory of the district . Tenants in common ,
joint tenants with or without right of survivorship , and tenants
by the entireties shall be deemed one freeholder or property
owner .
(2) When the petition is filed with the district , said district
shall give notice of the filing by publication pursuant to KRS
Chapter 424. Within thirty days after the publication , any resident
or freeholder of the proposed construction subdistrict may file
objections and the district shall set the case for hearing within
thirty days . If the district finds that the establishment of the
construction subdistrict is reasonably necessary for the public
health, convenience and comfort of the residents of the subdistrict ,
it shall make an order establishing the construction subdistrict and
designating it by name and number .
(3) If the district finds that the construction subdistrict is
not necessary , it may dismiss the petition . If the district finds
that any part of the proposed territory will not be benefited , it
may strike such part. If the district strikes a certain portion of
the area, the signature of the freeholders of that portion shall not
be counted in determining whether the necessary twenty -five
percent have petitioned for the creation of the subdistrict . A copy
of the order of the board establishing a construction subdistrict
shall be published in accordance with chapter 424 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes .
(4) An order of the district rejecting or dismissing the peti-
tion shall be deemed a final order of the district appealable to
the circuit court under the procedure set forth in section 17 of
this act within sixty days . Appeals to the circuit court from the
order establishing a construction subdistrict or striking or refusing
to strike any territory from a construction subdistrict shall be
made only as provided in section 17 of this act and only after
following the procedures required in section 17 of this act.

Section 23. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
If bonds have been issued for any construction subdistrict
secured entirely or partially by the right to levy an assessment
as provided by sections 20 to 31 of this act and such bonds are
redeemed prior to maturity , bonds to refund same may be issued
secured solely by the revenue of said system in the manner pro-
vided in section 22 of this act.
Section 24. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
(1 ) The district is authorized to establish a schedule of
rates , rentals , and charges to be collected from all real property
within a construction subdistrict served by construction subdis-
trict facilities . This schedule shall be in addition to the charge
authorized by KRS 76.090 .
This schedule shall be determined for each construction sub-
district on the basis of one or more of the factors stated in sub-
section ( 1 ) of KRS 76.090 .
( 2) Before final adoption of such a schedule the district
shall give notice of it pursuant to KRS Chapter 424. The first
notice shall be dated as of the date of the first publication ; that
notice shall state that the proposed or revised schedule of rates ,
rentals, and charges will remain open for inspection in the office of
the district for thirty days from the date in the notice , and that any
person claiming to be aggrieved by the proposed schedule may
file written objections to it with the district . The district shall
examine and hear any and all such objections , may modify the
proposed schedule, and shall adopt and establish a final schedule
within sixty days after the date of the first notice .

CHAPTER 33 135

is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The district may issue for one or more construction

may also be used to finance construction of a treatment plant
disposal plant , or outfall sewer of the type described in the
preceding sentence .
( 2 ) Bonds issued pursuant to subsection ( 1 ) shall be con-

for which they are issued .
( 3 ) Except as provided in this section , bonds authorized
by subsection ( 1 ) shall be governed by sections 22 and 25 of
this act . Such bonds shall be construction subdistrict bonds with-
in the meaning of paragraph ( a) of subsection ( 3 ) of section 24
of this act . They shall not be considered construction subdistrict
bonds within the meaning of subsection ( 6 ) of section 24 of
this act .

The district shall wind up the affairs of a construction sub-

Section 30.
Anew section
ofthe Kentucky Revised Statutes
iscreated to read as follows
:

140
ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

The establishment, financing and operation of a construction subdistrict by the district are in all respects in the interest of public health and for a public purpose. In so doing the district performs a governmental function in the exercise of the powers conferred upon it, and it shall be required to pay no taxes or assessments upon any property owned or acquired by it, or upon its activities in the operation and maintenance of its subdistrict facilities. Construction subdistrict bonds and the income therefrom are exempt from taxation.

Section 31. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is created to read as follows:

In the financing of a construction subdistrict the district may use the authority and procedures granted to incorporated municipalities by KRS 107.010 to 107.220. When applied to construction subdistricts, terms used in KRS 107.010 to 107.220 shall be construed to mean the following: "city" means "metropolitan sewer district"; "ordinance" means "resolution"; "clerk" means "secretary-treasurer of district"; "governing body" means "metropolitan sewer district board"; and "mayor or chairman of board of directors" means "chairman of metropolitan sewer district board". Bonds issued pursuant to this section are construction subdistrict bonds and obligations secured by assessments.

Section 32. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is created to read as follows:

The provisions of section 31 of this act, permitting financing subdistrict construction under the provisions of KRS 107.010 to 107.220, shall not repeal or reduce any existing rights or duties of the district, but shall constitute an additional method of financing.

Section 33. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is created to read as follows:

After the creation of a construction subdistrict under the provisions of sections 12 to 16 of this act, the board of the metropolitan sewer district may, if it deem it advisable, use the authority and procedures granted to sanitation districts by KRS 220.535 to 220.537 to annex territory to a subdistrict, "board of directors" being read as "metropolitan sewer district board."

CHAPTER 33 141
Section 34. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Language in KRS 220.535 , limiting the powers of annexation
to a sanitation district in a county not containing a city of the
first class , shall not be applicable to a metropolitan sewer district
which might use this method of annexation to a construction sub-
district even if it is located in a county containing a city of the
first class , and the Director of Sanitary Engineering of the De-
partment of Health shall function in regard to annexations by it
in the same manner and under the same procedures as set out in
KRS 220.535 to 220.537 as he would in his capacity as Commis-
sioner of Sanitation Districts for any sanitation district .
Section 35. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
The provisions of sections 33 and 34 of this act shall not
repeal or reduce any existing rights or duties of metropolitan
sewer districts , but shall constitute merely a procedure for annexa-
tion to construction subdistricts by a metropolitan sewer district .
Section 36. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
After the creation of a construction district under the pro-
visions of sections 12 to 16 of this act, the board may , if the board
deem it feasible , instead of issuing bonds as provided in section
21 of this act, issue apportionment warrants , using the procedures
after assessment set out in KRS 184.150 to KRS 184.250 , "road
district" being read as "metropolitan sewer district " , "board of
directors " being read as "metropolitan sewer district board " and
the reference to roads being read as reference to sewers and drains
including treatment plants .
Section 37. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
(1) If the district finds as a fact that the methods of financ-
ing sewer facilities within a construction subdistrict provided for
in this act will be inadequate , uneconomic , or unduly burden-
some to the residents to be initially served, the district may con-
tract with one or more persons to construct or cause to be con

142 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
structed a sewer system within the construction subdistrict or to
advance or loan money to the district for the construction of a
sewer system within the construction subdistrict . The sewer
system constructed will be the property of the construction sub-
district and will be a construction subdistrict facility. When the
district finds that one or more financing methods for construction
subdistricts established by this act, excluding this section, have
become feasible , the district may adopt such method or methods
to raise the money to pay for the construction of sewer facilities
within the construction subdistrict or to pay the loan .
( 2) The contract may be made prior to the making of an
order establishing the construction subdistrict . The contract may
require that one or more persons agree to buy construction sub-
district bonds to be issued pursuant to section 22 of this act in an
amount specified , which shall be sufficient to finance the con-
struction of the sewer system . The maximum price and yield of
the bonds shall be stated in the contract . Such contract shall not
prevent the district from selling the bonds to a lower bidder .
(3 ) All contracts made pursuant to this section shall be in
writing and shall contain a covenant that this section shall prevail
over any contrary feature of the contract .
(4) The General Assembly declares that the public policy
of the Commonwealth will not be offended merely because a
contract or loan made pursuant to this section is privately negoti-
ated or because it is made without competitive bidding.
(5) Bonds sold to one or more contracting parties pursuant
to subsection ( 2 ) shall be indorsed to disclose the nature of the
sale . Such bonds , no matter who the holder or owner , shall never
be eligible to have any payment made on their account pursuant
to subsection ( 6 ) of section 24 of this act .
Section 38. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Sewer and drainage systems or both constructed by construc-
tion subdistricts shall be operated and maintained by the district
and payment for the actual cost, including overhead and adminis-
trative costs , shall be paid by the districts from funds of the con-
struction subdistricts . Where sewer system constructed by con-

Section 43. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) In order to provide for the construction of a compre-
hensive sewage and sewage treatment system , or storm water and
surface drainage system , or both , the sanitation tax district and
a metropolitan sewer district may agree in writing that the latter
shall construct the system and operate it in consideration that the
sanitation tax district lease the system for a term of one year ,
which term may be renewed . No such system shall include lateral
sewers . It may include any other appliance or structure reason-
ably incident to such a system .
( 2 ) The sanitation tax district shall have an exclusive op-
tion to renew for each year succeeding the original term, but the
option shall not grant exclusive renewal options for more than
forty years . The method of exercising the exclusive option to
renew the lease shall be any method permissable under law which
does not have the effect of authorizing or permitting the sanita-
tion tax district to become indebted to an amount exceeding , in
any year , the income and revenue provided for such year within
the meaning of section 157 of the Constitution of Kentucky .
(3 ) A metropolitan sewer district is authorized to issue
bonds secured solely by rentals payable under such a lease to
finance construction of the system . Except as provided in the
preceding sentence ,
allmatters pertaining
tosuch bonds shall
be
governed by sections 22 and 25
ofthis act
.

CHAPTER 37
(Н. В. 4)
AN ACT providing a Uniform Act on Paternity .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 37 153
Section 1. The father of a child which is or may be born
out of wedlock is liable to the same extent as the father of a child
born in wedlock , whether or not the child is born alive , for the
reasonable expense of the mother's pregnancy and confinement
and for the education , necessary support and funeral expenses of
the child.
Section 2. Paternity may be determined upon the complaint
of the mother , child, person or agency substantially contributing
to the support of the child. Such action shall be brought by the
county attorney upon the request of such complainant herein
authorized . If paternity has been determined or has been
acknowledged according to the laws of this state , the liabilities of
the father may be enforced in the same or other proceedings
( 1 ) by the mother , child, person or agency substantially con-
tributing to the cost of pregnancy , confinement , education , neces-
sary support or funeral expenses .
Section 3. The father's liabilities for past education and
necessary support are limited to a period of four years next pre-
ceding the commencement of an action . An action under this
Act shall be brought within four years after the birth , miscarriage
or stillbirth of a child or be forever barred .
Section 4. The obligation of the estate of the father for
liabilities under this act is limited to amounts accrued prior to his
death and such sums as may be payable for dependence under
other laws .
Section 5. The county court has jurisdiction of an action
brought under this Act and
allremedies for the enforcement
of
judgments for expenses
ofpregnancy and confinement for
awife
orfor education
, necessary support
orfuneral expenses for
illegitimate children
. An appeal may be had
tothe circuit
court
ifprosecuted within sixty days from the date
ofjudgment
.
The court has continuing jurisdiction
tomodify
orrevoke
ajudg-
ment for future education
. All remedies under the Uniform
Reciprocal Enforcement
ofSupport Act are available for enforce-
ment
ofduties
ofsupport under this Act
.

upon all the evidence . If the experts conclude that the blood tests
show the possibility of the alleged father's paternity , admission
of this evidence is within the discretion of the court , depending
upon the infrequency of the blood type .
Section 12. Judgments under this Act may be for periodic
payments which may vary in amount . The court may order pay-
ments to be made to the mother or to some person, corporation , or
agency designated to administer them under the supervision of
the court. Wilful failure of the alleged father to perform the
judgment of the court may be punished as a contempt of the
court . The court may impose a punishment of not more than
thirty ( 30 ) days imprisonment in the county jail without the
intervention of a jury. This provision shall not be deemed to
limit or abrogate any other remedies which exist for the enforce-
ment of judgments .
Section 13. The court at any time may require the alleged
father to give bond or other security for the payment of the
judgment . If the court requires that bond be given and if the
alleged father is unwilling or unable to procure same , the alleged
father may be confined to the county jail until same is procured
provided , however , that after the alleged father has remained in
jail for a period of thirty ( 30 ) days he shall be released provided
he make oath in writing before the committing judge that he is
unable to obtain the execution of the bond .
Section 14. An agreement of settlement with the alleged
father is binding only when approved by the court .
Section 15. An action under this Act may be brought in
the county where the alleged father is present or has property
or in the county where the mother resides .
Section 16. This Act shall be so interpreted and construed
as to effectuate

CHAPTER 38
(Н. В. 5)
AN ACT relating to disposition of state or federally donated commodities
and prescribing penalties for the violation thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Whoever willfully sells , trades , barters, gives away or makes ,
or assists in making any unauthorized disposition of any food com-
modity donated under any program of the state or Federal
Government , or shall withhold or threaten to withhold from or
offer to provide the commodities to any person in order to in-
fluence the vote of any person at an election of public office , or
whoever , not being an authorized recipient thereof , willfully
converts to his own use or benefit any such food commodity , shall ,
upon conviction thereof , be punished by a fine of not less than
$100 or more than $500 , or by imprisonment for not less than 30
days or more than six months , or both .
Signed March 17, 1964
CHAPTER 39
(Н. В. 15)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Cold Spring , in Camp-
bell County .
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Cold Spring , in Camp-
bell County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the
fifth class ,
NOW, THEREFORE ,

CHAPTER 40 157
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
The City of Cold Spring , in Campbell County , is transferred
from the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Section 2. This Act shall become effective on the first day
of January, 1965 .
Signed March 17, 1964
CHAPTER 40
( Н. В. 38 )
AN ACT relating to the licensing of motor vehicles and operators .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 186.414 is amended to read:
Applications for renewal licenses shall be according to the
following schedule :
( 1 ) Persons whose family names begin with the letters "A"
through “K” inclusive , shall before August 1, 1958 , apply at the
office of the circuit court clerk in the county in which he resides ,
and make application upon a form furnished by the department
for a renewal license, which will expire on his birth month in the
year 1960 , and shall be renewed every two years during the
applicant's birth month thereafter .
(2) Persons whose family names begin with the letters
"L" through "Z" inclusive shall , before August 1, 1959 , apply at
the office of the circuit court clerk in the county in which he
resides , and make application upon a form furnished by the
department for a renewal license, which will expire on his birth
month in the year 1961 , and shall be renewed every two years
during the applicant's birth month thereafter .
(3) Any person who is a resident of this state and who has
obtained an operator's license in the manner provided for in this
chapter , which has not been cancelled , suspended or revoked for

158 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
any reason provided for in this chapter , and who is in the active
service of the armed forces of the United States shall , upon
written application to the circuit court clerk of the county in
which he resides , be granted an extension of his operator's license
beyond the periods set forth in subsections ( 1) and ( 2) of this
section . Upon application the circuit court clerk shall issue to
the applicant a written certification of operator's license extension .
The extension so granted shall be effective so long as the
applicant is an active member of the armed forces or six years
from the date of the application whichever is less .
Signed March 17, 1964

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 161.720 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The term "teacher" for the purpose of KRS 161.720 to
161.810 shall mean any person for whom certification is required
as a basis of employment in the public schools of the state with
the exception of the superintendent .
(2 ) The term "year " as applied to terms of service means
actual service of not less than seven school months within a
school year ; provided , however , that any board of education may
grant a leave of absence for professional advancement with full
credit for service .
(3) The term "limited contract " shall mean a contract for
the employment of a teacher for a term of one year only or for
that portion of the school year that remains at the time of em-
ployment . [four years or less ]
(4) The term "continuing service contract " shall mean
a contract for the employment of a teacher which shall remain

reaches the age of 65, or until it is terminated or suspended as
provided in KRS 161.790 and 161.800 .

teacher under a continuing contract .
(6 ) The term "standard " or "college" certificate for the
purpose of KRS 161.720 to 161.810 shall mean any certificate
issued upon the basis of graduation from a standard four -year
college .

CHAPTER 43
(Н. В. 72
)

' Retirement System of the State of
Kentucky

CHAPTER 43 167
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 161.220 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) "Retirement system " means the arrangement provided
for in KRS 161.230 to 161.710 for payment of [ retirement ] allow-
ances [ or disability allowances ] to teachers .
(2 ) "Retirement allowance " means the amount annually
payable during the course of his natural life to a member who has
been retired by reason of [ superannuation ] service .
( 3 ) "Disability allowance " means the amount annually
payable [ before the deductions authorized in KRS 161.670 during
the course of his natural life] to a member [who has been ]
retired by reason of disability [before reaching the age of sixty
years ] .
(4) "Teacher ” means :
( a) Any full-time regular or special teacher , principal ,
supervisor , superintendent , assistant superintendent , librarian ,
attendance officer , or other full -time member of the teaching or
professional staff engaged in the service of the public elementary
and secondary schools for whom certification is required as a
condition of employment ;
(b) All members of the instructional and administrative
staffs of Eastern Kentucky State College , Kentucky State College ,
Morehead State College , Murray State College , Western Kentucky
State College , Kentucky School for the Deaf, Kentucky School for
the Blind , and the State and area vocational schools [ West
Kentucky Vocational Training School and Mayo State Vocational
School ] who are regularly employed by a board of education ,
board of regents [or board of trustees for a full term for
which school is maintained ; ] or similar body ;
( c ) The Superintendent of Public Instruction and the
division heads and other professional staff members of the State
Department of Education ;
(d) Any member of a local retirement system who became
a member of the state teachers ' retirement system according to the
provisions of Acts , 1938 , 1st ex.s. , c. 1, paragraph 49; [ and ]

168 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( e ) Such members of the administrative staff of the
teachers ' retirement system of the State of Kentucky as the board
of trustees may designate by regulation ; and
(f) All members of the instructional and administrative staffs
of any existing or later established public junior college not
under control of the Board of Trustees of the University of
Kentucky .
( 5) "Present teacher" means any teacher who was a teacher
on or before July 1, 1940 , and became a member of the retirement
system created by Acts 1938 , 1st ex.s. , c. 1, on the date of the
inauguration of the system or within one year after that date , and
any teacher who was a member of a local teacher retirement
system in the public elementary or secondary schools of the state
on or before July 1, 1940 , and continued to be a member of such
system until he, with the membership of the local retirement
system , became a member of the state teachers ' retirement system ,
or who becomes a member under the provisions of KRS
161.470 (4).
( 6) "New teacher" means any teacher not a present teacher .
(7 ) “Prior service ” means the number of years during which
the member was a teacher in Kentucky prior to July 1 , 1941 , [ as
allowed in his prior service certificate ] except that not more than
thirty years' prior service shall be allowed or credited to any
teacher.
( 8 ) "Subsequent service" means the number of years during
which the teacher is a member of the [ state ] teachers ' retire-
ment system after July 1, 1941 .
[ ( 9 ) "Prior service certificate ” means the certificate issued
by the board of trustees of the retirement system designating the
amount of accredited service, not to exceed thirty years , of a
member prior to July 1, 1941. ]
[(10 )] (9) "Annual salary " means the average annual
salary which the member has received for his service as a teacher
in the public schools of Kentucky for five years or for as many
years, if less than five prior to July 1, 1941 , except that any
salary which exceeds two thousand dollars shall be considered two
thousand dollars . [ and during the five years or as many years if

retirement or annuity system created in any public school district
in Kentucky in accordance with the laws of Kentucky .
[ ( 18 ) ] ( 17) "Fiscal year " means the twelve -month period

[ ( 19) ] ( 18 ) "Public schools ” means the schools and other
institutions mentioned in subsection ( 4) of this section .
[ ( 20) ] (19) “Dependent " as used in KRS 161.520 and
Section 22 of this Act means a [ beneficiary of a survivors benefit ]
person who was receiving , at the time of death of the member , at
least one-half of the support from the member for maintenance ,
including board , lodging , medical care and like costs .
(20) “ Last earned annual compensation " means the gross
compensation before any deductions earned by the member for the
last full payroll period , multiplied by the number of payroll
periods in the member's contract year .

liabilities at those given periods .

the last reporting period must be at least twice the amount of the
proposed bond issue under the lease agreement .

end of the most recent reporting year must have been at least

of the accumulated contributions a survivors benefit , payable as

( a) Where the survivor is a widow or a dependent
widower , and the survivor's marriage to the deceased teacher

benefit shall be eighty dollars per month , or one -half of the
retirement allowance that the teacher would have received had
he been eligible and retired at the date of his death or was
receiving as a disabled teacher at the date of death , whichever
is larger .
( b ) Where the survivor is an unmarried child under age
eighteen , the benefit shall be eighty dollars per month in the
case of one such child, one hundred and twenty -five dollars per
month in the case of two such children , one hundred and fifty
dollars per month in the case of three such children , and one
hundred and sixty dollars per month in the case of four or more
such children .
( c) Where the survivor is a child age eighteen or older
whose mental or physical condition is sufficient to cause his
dependency on the deceased member at the time of the member's
death , the benefit shall be one hundred dollars per month , payable
for the life of such child or until such time as the mental or phys-
ical condition creating the dependency no longer exists . Benefits
under this paragraph and paragraph ( b ) shall apply to legally
adopted survivors provided the proceedings for the adoption
were initiated at least one year prior to the death of the member .
Benefits under this paragraph and paragraph ( b ) shall apply
notwithstanding an eligible widow or dependent widower sur-
vives the deceased member .

are dependent [parent ] parents aged sixty-five or over, the
benefit shall be eighty dollars per month for one parent or one
hundred and twenty dollars per month for two parents . De-
pendency of a parent shall be established as of the date of the
death of the member .
( e ) Where the sole eligible survivor is a dependent brother
or sister , the benefit shall be eighty dollars per month . In order
to qualify the brother or sister must have been a resident of the
deceased member's household for at least one full year prior to

CHAPTER 43 183
the member's death or must have been receiving care in a hos-
pital, nursing home or other institution at the member's expense
for a like period .
(f ) The benefit to a child shall terminate upon the attain-
ment of age eighteen or upon marriage . The benefit to a widow ,
dependent widower, dependent parent or dependent brother or
sister shall terminate upon marriage . The benefit shall not be
paid during the time the survivor , other than a child , is receiving
an annuity , pension or allowance in excess of eighty dollars per
month from a retirement or pension system in the state of Ken-
tucky financed in whole or part by public contributions , excluding
Old Age and Survivors Insurance , or has income from all sources
of $ 2,400 per year , not including any benefit payable under the
terms of KRS 161.220 to 161.710 . If income from all sources
exceeds $ 2,400 per year , one dollar shall be subtracted from the
benefit for each dollar of total income exceeding $2,400 per year .
( g ) The board of trustees shall be the sole judge of eligi-
bility or dependency of any beneficiary , and may require formal
application or information relating thereto , including proof of
annual income satisfactory to the board . The board of trustees
may subpoena records and individuals whenever it deems such
action necessary .
(h) No payments of benefits shall be made unless the
board of trustees shall have authorized such payments . The
board shall formulate and adopt necessary rules and regulations
for the administration of the foregoing provisions and in every
case the decision of the board of trustees shall be final as to
eligibility , dependency or disability , and the amounts of benefit
payable .

Section 18. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any member who has completed ten or more years of
accredited service in the public schools , or other covered posi-
tions , of the State of Kentucky , one full year of which immedi-
ately preceded retirement , may retire for disability and shall be
granted a disability allowance . The annual allowance shall be
equal to one-half of the last earned annual compensation .
( 2) No member who has reached the age of sixty years and
who is otherwise qualified for retirement may retire by reason of
disability.
(3 ) Members shall earn one year of entitlement to dis-
ability retirement for each four (4 ) years of service in a covered
position but any member meeting the service requirement for

CHAPTER 43 189
disability retirement shall be credited with no less than five
years of such eligibility .
(4 ) A member retired by reason of disability shall continue
to earn service credit at the rate of one year for each year so re-
tired .
(5) Any member retired by reason of disability and re-
maining disabled at the expiration of the eligibility period shall
be entitled to retire for service . The retirement allowance shall
be calculated as set forth in KRS 161.620 except that those per-
sons less than sixty years of age shall be considered as sixty.
(6) In order to qualify for retirement by reason of dis-
ability a teacher must suffer from a physical or mental condition
of such a nature as to render him incapable of being gainfully
employed as a teacher . Such incapability must be revealed by a
competent examination by a licensed physician or physicians and
must be approved by the medical review board .
(7 ) A member retired by reason of disability shall be re-
quired to undergo periodic examinations at the discretion of the
board of trustees to determine whether the disability allowance
shall be continued . When examination and recommendation of
the medical review board indicates the disability no longer exists ,
the allowance shall be discontinued .
(8) No payment of a disability benefit shall be made until
ninety ( 90 ) days after the application for disability retirement
has been filed.
(9) No person who receives a disability allowance may
serve as a teacher either within or without the state of Kentucky .
So doing shall constitute a misdemeanor . The recipient of a dis-
ability allowance who engages in any gainful occupation other
than teaching must make a report of the duties involved , com-
pensation received and any other pertinent information required
by the board of trustees. If the income from other employment
plus the disability benefit exceed seventy-five percent of the last
earned annual compensation , the benefit shall be reduced to an
amount which with earned income will not exceed seventy-five
percent of the last earned annual compensation . In no case shall
the benefit be reduced to less than fifty ( $50 ) per month .

190 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 10) The board of trustees shall designate a medical re-
view board consisting of three licensed physicians and may des-
ignate physicians in various areas of the Commonwealth to con-
duct examinations as needed. The medical review board shall

Section 21. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Any member of the teachers ' retirement system who enters
employment covered by another retirement system in Kentucky
financed in whole or part with public funds may retain member-

system . Persons who enter service covered by the teachers ' retire-

retain membership in that system providing the statutes and
regulations governing said system make continued membership
possible .
Section 22. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Upon the death of a member in active contributing service
at the time of death , who was eligible to retire by reason of serv-
ice, the spouse or legal dependent of said member , if named
as beneficiary , shall be entitled to elect , in lieu of a refund of
the member's account or benefits provided in KRS 161.520 (a), an
annuity actuarially equivalent at the attained age of the bene-

member had he been retired on the day immediately preceding

are qualifying for survivors benefits under the provisions of

CHAPTER 44
(Н. В. 95)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Stanton , in Powell
County . This Bill shall be known as "The B. E. "Nig ." Billings Bill " .
WHEREAS , information has been presented to the General Assembly
that the population of the city of Stanton , in Powell County , has
increased from 628 to 1,493 since the last session of the General
Assembly ; and
WHEREAS , the population of such City of Stanton is now such as to
justify its being classified as a city of the fifth class ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
The City of Stanton , in Powell County , is transferred from the
sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Signed March 17, 1964

CHAPTER 45
( Н. В. 100)
AN ACT relating to boards of waterworks of first class cities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 96.250 is amended to read:
The board of waterworks shall annually elect a president ,
treasurer , secretary and chief engineer , who shall hold their
offices for one year , or until their successors have been elected
and qualified , and devote all their time to the duties of their
respective offices . The member elected president shall have had
at least five years executive or major administrative experience
in public utility operation or administration . The offices of secre-
tary and treasurer may at the election of the board , be combined
in one person . No member of the board except the president shall
receive a salary , but the board may allow reasonable compensation
to each member other than the president for attendance upon the
meetings of the board [ , not exceeding ten dollars for each meet-
ing attended ] . The president shall be elected from the members
of the board . The president , secretary and treasurer , or the secre-
tary-treasurer if the offices are combined in one person , shall
each give bond, with approved surety, in an amount fixed by the
board , payable to the board and conditioned that the makers will
faithfully perform the duties of their several offices . The board
may appoint such other employes as it deems necessary or proper
and may fix the compensation of
itsofficers and employes
.
Signed March 17
, 1964

CHAPTER 50
(Н. В. 161 )
AN ACT relating to owner or tenant relocation required by road con-
struction .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. As a part of the cost of construction of a road ,
the Department of Highways may compensate resident owners or

212 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tenants for their reasonable and necessary moving expenses
caused by their displacement from real property acquired for
such road project . Relocation payments shall be subject to such
rules and regulations the Commissioner of Highways is hereby
authorized to prescribe , and payments shall not exceed $200 in
the case of an individual or family or $3,000 in the case of a
business concern , which may include the operation of a farm , a
nonprofit organization , and other activities which the Department
of Highways may by regulation define as a business concern . In
the case of a business concern , the allowable expenses for trans-
portation under this section shall not exceed the cost of moving
50 miles from the point from which such business concern is being
displaced . Such rules and regulations may include provisions
authorizing payments made to individuals and families of fixed
amounts , not to exceed $200 , in lieu of their respective reasonable
and necessary moving expenses .
Section 2. WHEREAS , there are now pending many ac-
quisitions of property whose owners whould be denied benefits
provided for in this Act , an emergency is declared to exist, and
this Act shall become effective upon
itspassage and approval by
the Governor
.
Signed March 17
, 1964

CHAPTER 52
(Н. В. 194)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Benham , in Harlan
County .
Whereas , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Benham , in Harlan
County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the
fifth class ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
The City of Benham , in Harlan County, is transferred from
the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Signed March 17, 1964
