CHAPTER 55
(Н. В. 210 )
AN ACT relating to the Department of Libraries .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 171.203 is amended to read:
Any funds appropriated for the purposes of KRS 171.201 to
171.205 shall be expended for aiding in the establishment , exten-
sion and development of local public library facilities [ ( exclusive
of library buildings , equipment and furniture ) ) through the pur-
chase of books , equipment or library buildings , or bookmobiles ,

CHAPTER 55 215
through technical assistance , educational and promotional pro-
grams , and such other methods and services as will best accom-
plish the purpose . Funds in the Public Library Service Fund shall
be paid out by the State Treasurer on warrants drawn by the
Commissioner of Finance on order of the State Librarian .
Section 2. KRS 171.204 is amended to read :
To the extent of funds available , grants shall be made to
counties applying for state aid, based upon population and
areas needed to be served and according to formulas pro-
vided for in the Federal Library Service Act . The Department of
Libraries may provide partial grants or loans for constructing
county or district library buildings and may accept federal funds
to be used for this purpose . [ in the order in which they meet the
requirements established by the State Librarian. Grants -in- aid
thus provided shall not exceed $5,000 to any county . Each county
receiving a state grant shall expend from local funds , for county-
wide public library service during the years for which aid is
received a sum equal to at least five percent of the amount re-
ceived from state funds ; the ratio of matching to be based on local
financial ability as determined by the assessed valuation of the
county .]
Section 3. KRS 173.360 is amended to read :
( 1 ) After the legislative body of a governmental unit has
made provisions for library service according to any of the
methods set forth in KRS 173.310 , the legislative body shall ap-
propriate money annually to furnish such service . In library re-
gions it shall not be less than three cents, nor more than ten cents
on each one hundred dollars worth of property assessed for local
taxation . In counties containing a city of the first class it shall
be not more than fifteen cents on each one hundred dollars worth
of property assessed for local taxation . In all other governmental
units it shall be not less than five cents nor more than fifteen cents
on each one hundred dollars worth of property assessed for local
taxation . In those instances where county library service has been
established on the initiative of the Fiscal Court and when an
appropriation of less than the minimum amount required by this
subsection is proposed , the minimum amount of support for
county library service may be determined annually through a

216 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
mutual agreement of the county library board , the county fiscal
court , and the State Department of Libraries . This agreement
shall be reflected in the records of the legislative body of the
governmental unit making the appropriation . [It may be a
special levy if the constitutional limit has not already been levied
or this amount included in the general levy . ]
(2 ) All funds for the library shall be deposited monthly to
the credit of the library board . These funds shall not be used
for any but library purposes . The treasurer of the library board
shall be required to execute bond with good and sufficient surety
thereon for the faithful performance of his duties , the amount of
the bond to be fixed by the board. If the bond has a corporate
surety the premium shall be paid from the library fund. The
board shall have exclusive control of expenditures , subject to an
examination of accounts as may be required by the legislative
body, and money shall be paid only upon vouchers approved by
the board . The board shall not make expenditures or incur in-
debtedness in any year in excess of the amount of money ap-
propriated and available for library purposes , except where a
library board is the owner of real estate not used for library pur-
poses, in which case they may borrow money secured solely by
that property . Principal and interest on indebtedness on real
estate owned by a library board not used for library purposes is
not chargeable to the library funds derived from taxation .

CHAPTER 59
(Н. В. 312)
AN ACT relating to motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 186.040 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Upon receiving the application and fee , the county
clerk shall issue to the owner a registration receipt containing the
information required by subsection ( 2 ) and a registration plate .
If the department finds that there is a shortage of materials
suitable for making plates , or that a substantial saving will result ,
it may require by regulation with the approval of the Governor
that previously issued plates continue to be used for a designated
period . For services performed , the owner shall pay the county
clerk the sum of fifty cents for each registration in addition to
the registration fee .
(2) The registration receipt shall contain the registration
number , the names and post office addresses of the owner and
the secured party [and of the lien holder ] as shown by the
financing statement [chattel mortgage , or other instrument con-
strued as such in this state ,] on file in the office of the county
clerk and such other information as the department may require .
The owner's copy of such receipt shall bear the seal of the county
clerk who issued same . No fee shall be charged for placing such
seal on the owner's copy of the receipt . [The county clerk shall
be paid a fee of sixty cents for recording each lien on the registra-
tion receipt in addition to statutory recording fees for recording
the chattel mortgage , or other instrument construed as such in
this state .]

CHAPTER 59 221
(3 ) If a motor vehicle , other than a new motor vehicle , is
registered in this state under the authority of a bill of sale , regis-
tration receipt or title , the registration receipt shall show the title
or the registration number and the name of the state issuing same
under which the motor vehicle was last registered in addition to
the other required information .
Section 2. KRS Chapter 186 is amended by inserting
therein a new section reading as follows :
( 1 ) Financing statements relating to vehicles required to
be registered in Kentucky by the county clerk shall be filed in the
office of the county clerk of the county in which the debtor
resides or , if the debtor is a nonresident , in the office of the county
clerk in which the vehicle is principally operated .
( 2) ( a ) Whenever a financing statement required by
KRS Chapter 355 relating to any vehicle registered or required
to be registered in Kentucky for use on the highway is presented
to a county clerk for filing, such clerk shall also immediately note
information required by the department relative thereto on the
owner's copy of the registration or transfer receipt issued for the
current registration period as noted in KRS 186.170 ( 2 ) , which
the secured party must obtain and present to the county clerk ,
along with the financing statement, within ten days after execu-
tion of the security agreement . The clerk shall also note such
information on the clerk's copy of the registration or transfer
receipt maintained in his office in numerical order . The clerk
noting the information on the owner's copy of the registration or
transfer receipt shall return such receipt to the owner within five
days after making such notation . For filing the financing state-
ment , the clerk shall collect a fee of $3.25 from the secured party
and , in addition , for noting the security interest on the required
receipts , the clerk shall collect from the secured party a fee of
eighty -five cents for each motor vehicle included on the financing
statement .
( b ) Whenever a financing statement affecting a vehicle is
presented to a county clerk for filing in any county other than
the one in which the current receipt was issued, the county clerk
shall , in addition to the other requirements of this section, mail
a notice of the security interest on a form furnished by the depart

issued. Upon receipt of said notice , the county clerk shall attach
it to the copy of the current receipt on file, maintained in
numerical order , in the county clerk's office and note the security
interest on the clerk's copy of the receipt .
(3 ) For failure to present both the current receipt and
financing statement within the time prescribed by subsection
2 ( a ) , the secured party shall pay a penalty of two dollars to the
county clerk as a prerequisite for noting the security interest on
the current receipt . The penalty , less the county clerk's com-
mission of five percent , shall be remitted to the department .
( 4) Whenever a perfected security interest is assigned , the
assignor shall immediately give a statement of assignment to the
assignee and at the same time deliver another copy of such state-
ment to the debtor . The assignee shall , within thirty days there-
after, present a copy of the statement of assignment to the county
clerk in whose office the financing statement is on file . Upon
receipt of the assignment, the county clerk shall mark the same
as provided in KRS 355.9-405 ( 2 ) and attach it to the original
financing statement. For his services the county clerk shall
collect a fee of seventy-five cents from the assignee .
(5 ) Whenever a security interest has been discharged ,
other than by proceedings under KRS 355.9-501 through
355.9-507 or similar proceedings , the secured party shall , within
thirty days thereafter , deliver a termination statement in the man-
ner required by KRS 355.9-404 to the county clerk of the county
in which the financing statement is filed. For failure to file the
termination statement within the allowable time , the secured

of this section . Within five days after the receipt of such docu-
ments , the county clerk shall note the filing in the index and , in
addition , note on the clerk's copy of the current receipt main-
tained in his office in numerical order , in language prescribed
by the department that the termination statement has been filed.
Upon presentation of the owner's copy of the receipt showing
such security interest, the clerk shall also note on such receipt
that the termination statement has been filed and place the seal
of the county clerk thereon . The clerk shall return the owner's
receipt to the owner . The county clerk shall remove the financ

or deliver it to the secured party along with any continuation

taining thereto . The county clerk shall then file the termination
statement in the place from which the financing statement was
removed . Termination statements must be retained in the clerk's
files for a period of two years subsequent to the date of filing

CHAPTER 60 227
CHAPTER 60
(H. R. 35 )
A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION relating to a memorial at Gettysburg
honoring soldiers of the Confederate States of America .
WHEREAS , after the death of the last Union veteran of the American
Civil War there was caused to be erected at the site of the battle of
Gettysburg a memorial honoring all Union soldiers , which memorial
was paid for by those States which furnished soldiers to the United
States Army ; and
WHEREAS , after the death of the last Confederate veteran of this great
conflict , the United Daughters of the Confederacy evidenced a desire
to cause to be erected a similar memorial at Gettysburg in honor
of all Confederate veterans, to be paid for by those States furnishing
troops to the Confederate States of America ; and
WHEREAS , the sum of $ 1,000 , representing the total contribution appor-
tioned to the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the Confederate memo-
rial , was appropriated from the contingency fund of former Governor
Bert Combs and deposited to the accounts of the Kentucky Historical
Society to be contracted to the United Daughters of the Confederacy
for the erection of this memorial ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. That the Kentucky Historical Society be author-
ized , by the approval of this resolution , to release and transfer to
the responsible person or persons the aforementioned sum of
$ 1,000 , for the sole and privileged purpose of erecting a suitable
and enduring memorial at Gettysburg to
allsoldiers
ofthe Confed-
eracy
, who by virtue
oftheir sacrifices and deaths
ontheir own
fields
ofhonor
-
in-defeat made America
astronger nation and
all subsequent American battlefields presidios for free men
of
all tenets throughout the world
.
Section
2. That
acopy
ofthis Resolution
beforwarded
to
Mrs. John Pierce Campbell
, Paducah
, Kentucky
, President
ofthe
Kentucky Division
, UDC
, for inclusion
in
itspermanent records
.
Signed March 17
, 1964

Section 2. Section 350.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The General Assembly finds that the unregulated strip
mining of coal and clay causes soil erosion , stream pollution, the
accumulation of stagnant water and the seepage of contaminated
water, increases the likelihood of floods , destroys the value of land
for agricultural purposes , counteracts efforts for the conservation
of soil , water and other natural resources , destroys or impairs the
property rights of citizens , creates fire hazards , and in general
creates hazards dangerous to life and property , so as to constitute
an imminent and inordinate peril to the welfare of the Common-
wealth . The General Assembly further finds that lands that
have been subjected to strip mining and have not been reclaimed
and rehabilitated in accordance with modern standards constitute
the aforementioned perils to the welfare of the Common-
wealth . The General Assembly further finds that there are wide
variations in the circumstances and conditions surrounding and
arising out of the strip mining of coal and clay due primarily to
difference in topographical and geological conditions , and by
reason thereof it is necessary , in order to provide the most effec-
tive , beneficial and equitable solution to the problem , that a
broad discretion be vested in the authority designated to adminis-
ter and enforce the regulatory provisions enacted by the General
Assembly . The General Assembly further finds that other com-
mercial activities involving the removal of minerals and other
natural substances other than coal and clay from the earth by
strip mining occasionally cause conditions or create results of the
character enumerated above, but that no imminent or inordinate
peril presently exists by reason thereof . Therefore , it is the pur-
pose of this chapter to provide such regulation and control of

230 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the strip mining of coal and clay as to minimize
itsinjurious
effects

CHAPTER 62
(S. B. 10)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Park Hills , in Kenton
County.
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Park Hills , in Kenton
County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the fourth
class ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
The City of Park Hills in Kenton County , is transferred from
the fifth to the fourth class of cities .
Signed March 19 , 1964
CHAPTER 63
( S. B. 11 )
AN ACT relating to the Thirtieth Circuit Court District ; creating two
additional common pleas divisions and the office of circuit judge for
each of the divisions established by this Act .
WHEREAS , the population of Jefferson County is now 652,400, and
WHEREAS , the 1960 census revealed that the population of Jefferson
County was 610,947, the present population constituting an increase
of 41,453 since 1960, and
WHEREAS , the five existing common pleas divisions of the Thirtieth
Circuit Court District have had an increasing number of cases which
is creating delay in bringing actions to trial, and

238 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , a survey of the judges of the Thirtieth Circuit Court District
conducted by the Louisville Bar Association indicates a serious need
for additional common pleas divisions in the district , and
WHEREAS , the population of Jefferson County justifies the addition of
at least two judges to the circuit court of the Thirtieth Circuit Court
District ,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Two judges of the circuit court for the Thirtieth
Circuit Court District are added to the district .
Section 2. Two divisions are added to the Common Pleas
Branch of the Circuit Court for the Thirtieth Circuit Court Dis-
trict , to be named the Common Pleas Branch Sixth Division ,
and the Common Pleas Branch Seventh Division . One of the
judges provided for in this Act shall be the judge of the Common
Pleas Branch Sixth Division and one shall be the judge of the
Common Pleas Branch Seventh Division .
Section 3. At the regular election to be held on the first
Tuesday after the first Monday in November , 1964 , there shall be
elected for the Thirtieth Circuit Court District a judge of the
Common Pleas Branch Sixth Division and a judge of the Com-
mon Pleas Branch Seventh Division . The elections shall be held
in accordance with the provisions of KRS 24.190 .
Section 4. The courts in the Thirtieth Circuit Court District
shall be held and the business therein conducted in the manner
provided by law.
Section 5. Whereas the public welfare demands that the
benefits of administration of justice without undue delay be
made available to the Thirtieth Circuit Court District, an emer-
gency is hereby declared to exist, and this Act shall become
effective upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
.
Signed March 19
, 1964

CHAPTER 65 239
CHAPTER 64
(S. B. 21 )

bell County .
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Alexandria , in Campbell
County is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the fifth
class,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
The city of Alexandria , in Campbell County, is transferred
from the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Section 2. This Act shall become effective on the first day
of January , 1965 .
Signed March 19 , 1964

CHAPTER 66
( S. B. 119 )
AN ACT relating to actions for libel and slander against newspapers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

242 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 1. ( 1 ) In any action for damages for the publica-
tion of a defamatory statement in a daily or other newspaper the
defendant shall be liable for actual damages sustained by plaintiff.
The defendant may plead the publication of a correction in
mitigation of damages . Punitive damages may be recovered only
if the plaintiff shall allege and prove publication with legal malice
and that the daily or other newspaper failed to make conspicuous
and timely publication of a correction after receiving a sufficient
demand for correction .
( 2 ) A "sufficient demand for correction " is a demand for
correction which is in writing ; which is signed by the plaintiff or
his duly authorized attorney or agent ; which specifies the state-
ment or statements claimed to be false and defamatory , states
wherein they are false, and sets forth the facts ; and which is de-
livered to the defendant prior to the commencement of the action .
( 3 ) A “correction " is either ( a ) the publication of an
acknowledgment that the statement or statements specified as
false and defamatory in the plaintiff's demand for correction are
erroneous , or ( b) the publication , in a fair and impartial manner
as a matter of law, of the plaintiff's statement of the facts ( as set
forth in his demand for correction ) or a fair summary thereof , ex-
clusive of any portions thereof which are defamatory of another ,
obscene , or otherwise improper for publication . If the demand
for correction has specified two or more statements as false and
defamatory , the correction may deal with some of such state-
ments pursuant to ( a ) above and with other of such statements
pursuant to ( b ) above .
(4) A “conspicuous publication " in a newspaper is a publi-
cation which is printed in substantially as conspicuous a manner
as the statement or statements specified as false and defamatory
in the demand for correction .
(5) A "timely publication " in a daily newspaper is a publi-
cation within three business days after the day on which a suf-
ficient demand for correction is received by the defendant . A
"timely publication " in a newspaper other than a daily newspaper
is a publication in or prior to the next regular issue which is
published not less than three business days after the day on which
a sufficient demand for correction is received by the defendant .

Section 9. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Any person , agency or municipality who violates any of the
provisions of KRS 104.400 , or who fails or refuses to obtain a
permit as provided herein , or who violates any determination or
order promulgated pursuant to the provisions of this Act shall
be liable to a civil penalty of not more than $1,000.00 for said

violation . Such penalties shall be recoverable in an action

Franklin Circuit Court or in the circuit court having jurisdiction

Attorney, the State police shall serve such orders , judgments

visions of this Act .

of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 71
(S. B. 163)
AN ACT relating to conservation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. ( 1 ) There is hereby created in the Depart-
ment of Conservation , a conservation board composed of seven
( 7 ) members appointed by the Governor upon the recommenda-
tion of the Commissioner of the Department of Conservation .
(2 ) The board shall be advisory to the Commissioner and
to the Department of Conservation . The members of the board
shall be citizens and residents of the state , selected with reference
to their training and experience in relation to the principal ac-
tivities required of the Department of Conservation .
(3 ) The term of office shall be for four (4 ) years con-
current with that of the Governor or until their successor has
been qualified .
(4) No member of the board shall be a candidate for or
hold any public office other than that of member of the board ;
nor shall he be a member of any committee of a political party .
Signed March 19, 1964
CHAPTER 72
(S. B. 168 )
AN ACT relating to appeals in criminal cases .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 21.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) A defendant may appeal as a matter of right to the
Court of Appeals from a final judgment of the circuit court
imposing a sentence of confinement or imprisonment of twelve
months or more .

254 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2) The Court of Appeals may grant an appeal on a motion
by a defendant from a final judgment of the circuit court im-
posing a sentence of confinement of less than twelve months
or a fine or both. The court may also grant an appeal on a
motion by a defendant from a final judgment of a county judge
on a prosecution transferred to the county judge in accordance
with KRS 23.145 .
(3) An appeal may be taken to the Court of Appeals by
the Commonwealth from an adverse decision or ruling of the
circuit judge and such an appeal shall not suspend the proceed-
ings in the case . An appeal by the Commonwealth shall be
taken in the manner provided in the Rules of Criminal Procedure
except that the record on appeal shall be transmitted by the
clerk of the circuit court to the Attorney General . If the At-
torney General is satisfied , after inspecting the record , that
error has been committed to the prejudice of the Commonwealth ,
upon which it is important to the correct and uniform admin-
istration of the law that the Court of Appeals should decide , he
may deliver the record to the Clerk of the Court of Appeals
within the time prescribed by the Rules of Criminal Procedure .
Section 2. Section 23.032 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The defendant may appeal from a judgment of a
county , quarterly , justice's or police court imposing a fine or
confinement , or both , to the circuit court of the county in which
the judgment was rendered .
( 2 ) The Commonwealth or city shall have no right of ap-
peal to the circuit court from judgments of courts of inferior
jurisdiction except that a city may appeal where the validity
of an ordinance is in issue . After a decision has been rendered
in the circuit court on the city's appeal either the city or the
defendant may appeal from the circuit court to the Court of
Appeals .
Signed March 19, 1964

CHAPTER 74
(
S.B. 176
)
AN ACT relating
tothe selection of juries in police courts of third class
cities
.

256 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is created
to read as follows :
( 1) In any county containing a city of the third class , the
judge of the police court or the judge assisted by three commis-
sioners as per KRS 29.055 ( 1 ) shall each year select a list of not
less than three hundred qualified citizens and residents of the
city and county the names of which can be obtained from the
tax records , voter registration lists or either of said sources for
service as police court jurors .
(2) The judge or his commissioners shall place the name
and address of each person so selected on a small piece of paper ,
each slip being as near the same size and appearance as prac-
ticable . Each slip shall then be deposited in a revolving drum .
The drum shall be emptied once a year prior to the depositing of
slips selected from the new list. The drum shall be kept locked
and the key to said drum shall be kept by the judge or clerk of
the police court .
( 3 ) Whenever a jury is needed for jury service, the judge
shall select at least twelve names from the drum and said persons
shall be summoned by a police officer of the city.
(4) In the event that the jury panel is exhausted prior to
the expiration of the time when the judge must draw a new list ,
then the judge may compel any police officer of the city to sum-
mon any number of additional qualified jurors as becomes neces-
sary.
(5) In the event the jury panel drawn for a particular trial
is exhausted then the judge may compel any police officer of
the city to summon additional qualified jurors to fill the panel .
( 6) If jury commissioners are used, they shall be compen-
sated at the rate of five dollars per day for the time actually
engaged, the claim for which shall be certified by the judge
appointing him or them and shall be paid by the city treasurer .
Signed March 19, 1964

CHAPTER 79
(S. B. 213 )
AN ACT relating to the establishment of regional community mental
health services programs and providing state grants - in - aid therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The Commissioner of Mental Health is hereby
authorized to make grants to assist any combination of cities and
counties , or non -profit corporations in the establishment and op-
eration of regional community mental health programs to provide
at least four of the following six services :
(1) collaborative and cooperative services with public
health and other groups for programs of prevention of mental
illness , mental retardation , and other psychiatric disabilities ;
( 2) informational and educational services to the general
public , and lay and professional groups ;

262 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3) consultative services to schools , courts and health and
welfare agencies , both public and private ;
(4) in-patient treatment services ;
(5) out-patient diagnostic and treatment services ;
( 6 ) rehabilitative services for patients suffering from men-
tal or emotional disorders , mental retardation or other psychiatric
conditions , particularly those who have received prior treatment

Section 8. Subject to the provisions of this section and the

(2) Recruit and promote local financial support for the

industrial and private foundations , voluntary agencies and other
lawful sources , and promote public support for municipal and
county appropriations .
(3 ) Promote , arrange and implement working agreements
with other social service agencies , both public and private , and
with other educational and judicial agencies .
(4) Advise the administrator of the community mental

to stimulate effective community relations .

mendations thereon ; and
( 6 ) When so determined by the authority establishing the
program , act as the administrator of the program .
Section 9. In addition to the powers and duties already
conferred upon him by the law, the Commissioner of Mental
Health shall :

of community mental health programs to receive state grants ,
prescribing standards for qualification of personnel and quality
of professional service and for in- service training and educational
leave programs for personnel , governing eligibility for service so
that no person will be denied service on the basis of race, color
or creed , or inability to pay, providing for establishment of fee
schedules which shall be based upon ability to pay, regulating
fees for diagnostic services which services may be provided for
anyone without regard to his financial status when referred by the

recommendations thereon to community mental health boards
and program administrators ;
(3 ) Provide consultative staff service to communities to
assist in ascertaining local needs and in planning and establishing
community mental health programs ; and
(4) Employ qualified personnel , including a director of
community mental health services , to implement this Act .

CHAPTER 81
(S. B. 229 )
AN ACT providing for the taking of the sense of the people of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky as to the necessity and expediency of calling
a Convention for the purpose of revising or amending the Constitu-
tion of the Commonwealth , and such amendments as may have been
made to the same , as provided by Section 258 of said Constitution .

CHAPTER 81 275
WHEREAS , there have been great and many changes in the government
of the people of this Commonwealth since the adoption of the present
Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in 1891 , and
WHEREAS , in response to an urgent need for a study and revision of
said Constitution , the 1964 General Assembly of Kentucky has here-
tofore enacted into law House Bill No. 39, An Act amending KRS
7.170 creating the Constitution Revision Assembly , and
WHEREAS , it is the function of said Constitution Revision Assembly to
carry on a program of study , review , examination and exposition of
the Constitution of Kentucky and to prepare and propose in detail
such drafts , amendments , or revisions of the Constitution as will
promote the interest and welfare of the Commonwealth , and
WHEREAS , said Constitution Revision Assembly has been duly ap-
pointed and has heretofore convened on February 17 , 1964 , and
reports of said assembly will be available for Convention considera-
tion before the preliminary steps prescribed in the present Consti-
tution for calling a Convention can be accomplished ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
(1 ) The sense of the people of the Commonwealth as to the
necessity and expediency of calling a Convention for the purpose
of revising or amending the Constitution of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , and such amendments as may have been made to
the same , shall be taken as herein provided and as provided by
Section 258 of said Constitution .
(2) Upon the passage of this Act by a majority of

CHAPTER 83
(Н. В. 27)
AN ACT relating to elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 160.220 is amended to read:
All elections for members of boards of education shall be
by secret ballot. The ballot shall be on a separate sheet from
all other ballots used in any election . The county clerk shall
cause to be printed on the ballot the names of legally eligible
candidates who have filed with the clerk a petition as pro-
vided in KRS 118.010 . [ in whose behalf he is petitioned to
do so in writing by not less than fifty legal voters of the
county school district division or independent school district as
the case may be, except that in independent school districts
embracing a city of the first or second class the number of

278 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
such petitioners shall not be less than one hundred . The
petitions must be filed as provided in KRS 118.120 and 118.130 .
Each petition must be signed by the requisite number of qualified
persons , and shall show the district or the division of the county
district , as the case may be , and the place of residence of each
person signing it. In county school district divisions no person
shall sign more than one petition , and in independent school
districts not more petitions than the number of offices to be
filled.]
Section 2. KRS 118.070 is amended to read:
(1) Except as provided in KRS 118.110 , and subject to the
provisions of subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3) of this section, the county
clerk of each county shall cause to be printed on the ballots for
the regular election the names of the following persons :
(a) Candidates of a political party , as defined in KRS
119.010 , who have received certificates of nomination at the
preceding primary election , or certificates of nomination under
KRS 119.090 , and whose certificates of nomination have been
filed with the clerk or the Secretary of State as provided in KRS
118.120 , 118.130 and 119.090 .
(b) Candidates of a political party , as defined in KRS
119.010 , who have been nominated for an unexpired term in a
manner determined by the governing authority of the party, as
provided in KRS 119.030 , and whose evidences of nomination
have been filed with the clerk or the Secretary of State within
the time prescribed in KRS 118.130 .
(c) Candidates of a political party , as defined in KRS
119.010 , who have been nominated by the governing authority
of the party to fill a vacancy in the candidacy of a person nomi-
nated at the preceding primary election , as provided in KRS
119.020 , and whose certificates of nomination have been filed
with the clerk or the Secretary of State, in sufficient time before
the regular election to enable their names to be printed on the
ballots at the time the ballots are required to be printed .
(d) Candidates who have been nominated by a political
organization as provided in KRS 118.090 , and whose certificates
or petitions of nomination have been filed with the clerk or the
Secretary of State within the time prescribed in KRS 118.130 .

CHAPTER 83 279
(e) Candidates who have been nominated by petition as
provided in KRS 118.080 , and whose petitions of nomination have
been filed with the clerk or the Secretary of State within the time
prescribed in KRS 118.130 .
(f) Candidates for President and Vice President of the
United States , of those political parties and organizations who
have nominated presidential electors as provided in KRS 118.090 ,
where the certificate of nomination of such electors has been filed
with the Secretary of State within the time prescribed in KRS
118.130.
(2) Those candidates whose petitions or certificates of nomi-
nation are required by law to be filed with the Secretary of State
shall have their names printed on the ballots when certified to
the county clerk as provided in KRS 118.150 .
(3) Candidates for members of boards of education shall
have their names printed on ballots for the regular election only
after filing [when nominated ] as provided in KRS 160.220 .
(4) Except as provided in KRS 119.020 and 119.030 , the
name of no candidate shall be printed upon the ballots for any
regular election as the nominee of any political party , as defined
in KRS 119.010 , or under the emblem of any political party , as
so defined , except those candidates who have been duly and regu-
larly nominated as nominees of that party at a primary election
held as provided in Chapter 119 .
(5) No county clerk shall knowingly cause to be printed ,
upon the ballot for any regular election , the name of any candi-
date of a political party , as defined in KRS 119.010 , who has not
been nominated in the manner provided in the primary election
laws .
Section 3. KRS 118.080 is amended to read:
( 1) [ except as provided in subsection ( 3 ) of this section,]
A candidate for any office to be voted for at any regular election
may be nominated by a petition of electors qualified to vote for
him , complying with the provisions of subsection (2) of this section .
( 2) [ Except as provided in subsection ( 3 ) of this section,]
A petition of nomination for a state officer , or any officer for

280 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
whom all the electors of the state are entitled to vote , shall con-
tain one thousand petitioners ; for a representative in Congress
from any Congressional district , or for any officer from an other
district except as herein provided , four hundred petitioners ; for a
county officer or member of the General Assembly , one hundred
petitioners ; for an officer of a precinct or ward , or other division
less than a county , twenty petitioners . The signatures of the
petition need not be appended to one paper . No petitioner shall
be counted unless his residence and postoffice address are desig-
nated. If any person joins in nominating , by petition , more
than one nominee for any office to be filled , he shall not be
counted as a petitioner for either nomination . The petition shall
state the name and residence of each of the candidates , that he
is legally qualified to hold the office , and that the subscribers
desire , and are legally qualified , to vote for the candidate . The
petition shall designate a brief name or title of the party or
principle that the candidate represents , together with any simple
figure or device by which it is desired that he be designated
on the ballots .
[(3) Candidates for members of boards of education shall
be nominated as provided in KRS 160.220 .]
Signed March 19, 1964
CHAPTER 84
(Н. В. 111 )
AN ACT relating to taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 92.510 is amended to read :
(1) The supervisors of taxes in each city of the fourth class
shall meet in the office of the assessor on the first Monday in May
of each year , and may meet at such time prior to the first Monday
in May as they are called upon to meet by the tax assessor in the
event the latter shall have completed the taking of the assessment

CHAPTER 85 281
list prior to the first Monday in May , pursuant to KRS 92.490 ,
and may adjourn from day to day until their work is completed ,
not exceeding twenty days . Ten days' notice of the place of
meeting shall be given by printed notice , and no assessment shall
be increased unless the taxpayer , if he lives in the city , has been
cited to appear before the supervisors . The assessor shall meet
with the supervisors .
( 2) The supervisors of taxes shall carefully examine the
assessor's books , and correct any errors of the assessor that are
found. They shall hear complaints of taxpayers , made in person
or by agent or attorney , and may add to, increase or decrease
any list of property or the value thereof , or change the name of
the person assessed .
(3 ) Failure or informality in the election of supervisors
of taxes , or in their meetings or proceedings , shall not affect the
validity of any tax .
(4) The provisions of subsection ( 2) of KRS 92.530 shall
govern appeals from the action of the supervisors of taxes in
cities of the fourth class .
Signed March 19, 1964
CHAPTER 85
(Н. В. 118)
AN ACT relating to the abuse or injury to children and the jurisdiction
to be assumed by County Court sitting in Juvenile Session .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The purpose of this Act is to provide for the
protection of children who have had physical injury inflicted
upon them and who are further threatened by the conduct of
those responsible for their care and protection and to bring said
cases under the purview of the county court sitting in juvenile
session . Physicians who become aware of such cases shall report
them to the appropriate police authority thereby causing the pro

282 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tective services of the State to be brought to bear in an effort to
protect the health and welfare of these children and to prevent
further abuses .
Section 2. ( 1 ) Any physician , osteopathic physician , or
other person having reasonable cause to suspect that a child
under the age of 18 brought to his attention has had serious phys-
ical injury or injuries inflicted upon him other than by accidental
means by a parent or other person responsible for his care, shall re-
port or cause reports to be made in accordance with the provisions
of this Act; provided that when the attendance of such a person
with respect to a child is pursuant to the performance of services
as a member of the staff of an institution he shall notify the
person in charge of the institution or his designated delegate who
shall report or cause reports to be made in accordance with the
provisions of this Act .
(2) An oral report shall be made immediately by telephone
or otherwise and followed as soon thereafter as possible by a
report in writing to an appropriate police authority , and a copy
thereof shall be forwarded to the Department of Child Welfare
for investigation . Such reports shall contain the names and ad-
dresses of the child and his parents or other persons responsible
for his care, if known , the child's age , the nature and extent of the
child's injuries ( including any evidence of previous injuries ) , and
any other information that the person believes might be helpful
in establishing the cause of the injuries and the identity of the
perpetrator .
(3 ) Anyone acting upon reasonable cause in the making of
a report pursuant to this Act shall have immunity from any liabil-
ity, civil
orcriminal
, that might otherwise
beincurred
orimposed
.
Any such participant shall have the same immunity with respect
toparticipation
inany such judicial proceeding resulting from
such report
.
(4) Neither the physician
-patient privilege nor the
husband
-wife privilege shall
be
aground for excluding evidence
regarding
achild's injuries
orthe cause thereof
,
inany judicial
proceeding resulting from
areport pursuant
tothis Act
.
(
5) Anyone knowingly and willfully violating the provi

CHAPTER 85 283
sions of Section 2 of this Act shall be punished by a fine of not
less than ten dollars nor more than one hundred dollars .
Section 3. Chapter 208.020 of KRS is amended to read as
follows :
(1) The juvenile session of the county court of each county
shall have exclusive jurisdiction in proceedings concerning any
child , living , or found , within the county and has not reached his
eighteenth birthday :
( a) Who has committed a public offense, except a moving
motor vehicle offense , involving a child sixteen years of age or
older . The court shall not have jurisdiction in any prosecution of
a moving motor vehicle offense involving a child sixteen years
of age or older . Juvenile offenders sixteen years of age or older
accused of moving motor vehicle offenses shall be treated as any
adult offender ; provided , however , that the term "moving motor
vehicle offense" shall not be deemed to include the offense of
stealing or converting a motor vehicle nor operating the same
without the owner's consent, nor any offense which constitutes
a felony ; or
(b) Who does not subject himself to the reasonable con-
trol of his parents , teacher , guardian or custodian , by reason of
being wayward or habitually disobedient ; or
(c) Who is an habitual truant from home or from school ; or
(d) Who is found by the court to be dependent , neglected ,
needy or abandoned .
( 2) The court of each county wherein a public offense ,
as defined in paragraph ( a) of subsection ( 1 ) of this section, is
committed by a child who is a resident of another county of this
state , shall have concurrent jurisdiction over such child with the
court of the county wherein the child resides , or the court of the
county where the child is found . Whichever court first acquires
jurisdiction of such child may proceed to final disposition of his

at least one of which is current service , may retire on or after his
fifty - fifth birthday provided that he shall not have withdrawn his
accumulated contributions prior to the effective date of his retire-
ment . The member shall file a written notification to the board
setting forth at what time the retirement is to become effective ,
provided that such effective date shall be subsequent to the filing
of such notice .
Section 4. Section 61.595 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Upon service retirement at normal retirement date ,
or subsequent thereto , a member may receive an annual retire-
ment allowance , payable monthly during his lifetime , which shall
consist of an amount equal to one percent of the first $4,800 of
final compensation multiplied by the number of years of service ,
plus one and one-fourth percent of final compensation in excess of
$4,800 multiplied by the number of years of service, provided
that the annual retirement allowance for each year a member has
served as a member of the General Assembly shall be not less than
$50 ; except that the amount of such annual retirement allowance
for a member with ten or more years of service, at least one of
which is current service shall not be less than $480 . [ , provided
that if such sum is greater than fifty -two percent of final com-
pensation , the annual retirement allowance shall be fifty -two
percent of final compensation ]
( 2) Upon service retirement prior to normal retirement
date , a member may receive an annual retirement allowance pay-
able monthly during his lifetime which shall be determined in the
same manner as for retirement at his normal retirement date with
years of service and final compensation being determined as of
the date of his actual retirement , but the amount of the retire

CHAPTER 87
(Н. В. 140)
AN ACT relating to credit unions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 290.110 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The fiscal year of every credit union shall end at the
close of business on December 31. The annual meeting of the
corporation shall be held prior to June 30 in each year [in Janu-
ary ] . Special meetings may be held by order of the directors
or of the supervisory committee, and shall be held upon request
in writing of ten percent of the members. Notice of all meetings
of the corporation shall be given in the manner prescribed in the
bylaws.
(2) At all meetings of members a member shall have but
one vote, irrespective of the number of shares held. No share-
holder may vote by proxy but a society , association, partnership or
corporation having membership in the corporation , may be
represented by one person authorized to so represent it .
( 3 ) At any meeting the members may decide upon any
question of interest to the corporation and may overrule the board
of directors . [ If the notice of the meeting shall have specified the
question to be considered , the corporation may , by a three-fourths
vote of the members present and represented, vote to amend the
bylaws . ]
Section 2. The preamble and subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
290.030 is amended to read as follows :
At the time of filing the articles of incorporation with the
director of banking the incorporators shall submit , in duplicate ,
a set of bylaws , with acknowledgment of their adoption by the
incorporators which shall provide :
( 1 ) The date of the annual meeting ; [which shall be in
January of each year ; ] the manner in which members shall be
notified of meetings ; the manner of conducting meetings ; the
number of members constituting a quorum with regulations as
to voting .
Signed March 19, 1964

CHAPTER 91 307
If a member has ceased to be an employe he , if living , if

Section 7. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) A member shall terminate employment on his normal
retirement date .
(2 ) Any member with at least five years of service credit
may retire at his normal retirement date , or subsequent thereto ,
upon written notification to the board , setting forth at what
time the retirement is to become effective , provided that such
effective date shall be after his last day of service and subsequent
to the filing of such notice .
( 3 ) The member shall have the right to elect to have his
retirement allowance payable under any one of the plans set
forth in this section . The member shall not have the right to
revoke such election after the initial benefit payment has been
drawn by the State Treasurer .
(4) The member may elect to receive an annual retire-
ment allowance , payable monthly during his lifetime , equal
to one and three- fourths percent of final compensation for each
year of service provided that such monthly payments shall be
reduced by eighty - five dollars beginning with the first payment
following his sixty-fifth birthday .
(5) The member may elect to receive a decreased level
monthly retirement allowance payable for life, actuarially
equivalent to the retirement allowance payable under subsection
(4) of this section .
( 6 ) The member may elect to receive a monthly retire-
ment allowance payable for ten years certain , actuarially
equivalent to the retirement allowance payable under subsection
(4) of this section .
(7) The member may elect to receive a decreased monthly
retirement allowance during his lifetime and have such retire

ment allowance continued after his death to his spouse or a
dependent receiving at least one-half of his support from the

payable under subsection ( 4 ) of this section . In case of such
an election , benefits that might otherwise be provided under
Section 6 of this Act shall not be payable upon the death of the
member .
Section 8. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1) If a member , eligible to retire on benefits payable as
provided in Section 7 of this Act, dies at any time before the
first benefit payment has been drawn by the State Treasurer
and has on file with the board at the time of his death a written
designation of a beneficiary , who is his spouse or a dependent

payable monthly which shall be equal to the benefit that would
have been paid had the member retired immediately prior to
his date of death and elected to receive payments under sub-

(2) If a member in service with fifteen or more years

and has on file with the board at the time of his death a written

commencing in the month following the member's death and

payments shall be actuarially equivalent to the benefit the
member would have been entitled to receive, based on his age ,
years of service and final compensation at the date of his death ,
had the member been eligible for retirement and had he chosen
benefits payable under subsection ( 7) of Section 7 of this Act .

Section 11. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) Partial disability as used in KRS 16.505 to 16.645 shall
mean such disability as results in a member's incapacity to con-

incapacitated such member and is the result of bodily injury or
disease . The board shall have full power and exclusive authority
to determine the existence of partial disability ; it shall establish
by appropriate findings utilizing consistent procedures , including
services of an examiner, the disability ratio applicable to such
disabled member .

this section, no injury shall be deemed to be disability if such
injury results directly or indirectly from :

(b) Injury or disease resulting from military service ; or
( c) Disease suffered or contracted prior to date of employ-
ment as an employe of the employer .

member who is age fifty -five or over .

CHAPTER 92
(Н. В. 325 )
AN ACT relating to public library districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act , unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) "District" means "public library district " ;
( 2 ) " Board " means the board of trustees of a public library
district ;
( 3 ) " County ” means a county not containing a city of the
first class .
Section 2. All of the territory in a county , or in two or
more counties contiguous to each other may be organized into
a public library district for the purpose of levying a tax to pay
for establishing , equipping , maintaining and administering
libraries , or for contracting for library service from any existing
library.
Section 3. Districts shall be organized in the following
manner:
( 1 ) Upon the filing of a duly certified petition of fifty- one
percent or more of the number of duly qualified voters who voted

316 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
in the last general election in each county to be included within
the territorial limits of the proposed district , the fiscal court of
each county in the proposed district shall adopt a resolution
ordering the levy of a tax of not more than twenty cents on
each one hundred dollars worth of property assessed for local
taxation . Such petition shall be filed with the fiscal court in
each county in the proposed district not later than ninety days
after the date of the first signature on the petition . A certified
copy of the order of the fiscal court shall be filed within thirty
days with the county court clerk who will add the levy to the
next annual tax bill of the county or counties concerned .
form:
( 2 ) The petition shall be in substantially the following
The following duly qualified voters of ( insert name of
county or counties ) hereby petition the fiscal court of each county
concerned to establish a public library district for ( insert name
of county or counties ) which shall have the authority to impose
a special ad valorem tax of ( insert exact amount ) on each one
hundred dollars worth of property assessed for local taxation
in the district for the maintenance and operation of the ( insert
name ) public library district .
(3 ) The petition shall contain the following : The name
and address of each petitioner and the date upon which he
signed the petition .
(4) All special ad valorem taxes authorized by this Act
shall be collected in the same manner as are other county ad
valorem taxes in each county affected and shall be turned over to
the board as the governing body of the district . The special ad
valorem tax shall be in addition to all other ad valorem taxes .
Section 4. Upon the creation of a district , the fiscal court
of each county in the district shall at once notify the Department
of Libraries of the establishment of the district . The Department
of Libraries shall then recommend to the county judge of each
county in the district the names of suitable persons from that
county to be appointed to the board . The Department of
Libraries in recommending persons to the county judge for ap-
pointment to the board shall recommend twice as many persons

CHAPTER 92 317
for each county as the county is entitled to have members ap-
pointed, and the county judge shall immediately make his
selection from those recommended . Where the district consists
of one county , the county judge shall appoint five persons from
that county as members . The Department of Libraries shall pre-
scribe by regulation the number of board members when the dis-
trict consists of more than one county , provided that the board
shall consist of not less than one nor more than four members
from each county , each county having such number of members

Section 19. A district may be dissolved in the following
manner :
( 1 ) Upon filing of a duly certified petition of fifty-one
percent of the number of qualified voters who voted in the last
general election in the district , the fiscal court of each county in
the district shall adopt a resolution to dissolve a library district .
( 2 ) The petition shall be in substantially the following
form: "The following qualified voters in ( insert name of county
or counties ) favor dissolving the ( insert name of district ) public
library district ." It shall be presented to the fiscal court within
ninety days after having been signed by the first petitioner .
(3 ) A certified copy of the order of the fiscal court shall
be filed with the county clerk .

upon remove the tax levy from the tax bills of the property own-
ers of the district and the district shall be dissolved .

CHAPTER 94
( Н. В. 350 )
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Edmonton , in Metcalfe
County .
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Edmonton , in Metcalfe
County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the fifth
class;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
The city of Edmonton , in Metcalfe County , is transferred
from the sixth to fifth class of cities .
Signed March 19, 1964

CHAPTER 96
(Η . Β. 392 )
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Definitions as used in this Act :
( 1 ) " Insurance company " means an insurer as defined in
KRS 304.003 . Such definition does not include :
( a) Societies , companies or associations organized under
the authority and patronage of any church or religious denomina-
tion for the exclusive purpose of insuring the property of
churches or religious denomination and the personal property or
lives of the pastors and ministers thereof .
(b) Fraternal benefit societies , hospital service corpora-
tions , medical service corporations , or burial associations .
( c) Domestic assessment or cooperative insurance com

CHAPTER 96 339
panies organized and operated under Chapter 299 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes .
( 2) "Person " means a person as defined in KRS 304.005 .
( 3 ) "Domestic company " means such company as defined
in KRS 304.060 ( 1 ) .
(4) "Unauthorized insurance company " means any insur-
ance company not authorized to do business in this state as pro-
vided in Chapter 304 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
(5) “Gross premiums " means all amounts charged , paid ,
received or credited in consideration for insurance and shall
include ordinary premiums , deposits , policy fees , membership
fees , application fees , amounts received for reinsurance on Ken-
tucky risks from unauthorized companies , and every other con-
sideration for or incidental to such insurance , including any
charges for services rendered in connection with such insurance .
(6 ) "Taxable premium " means gross premiums properly
allocable to this state less the deductions properly allocable to
this state for:
( a) Returns and refunds made or credited to policyholders
on cancelled policies but no deduction shall be allowed for any
dividends except accident and health dividends ; and
(b ) Policies not taken .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
(1 ) Any company whose tax, as provided in KRS 136.330 ,
136.340 , 136.350 , 136.370 and 342.445 or 304.110 , was five thou-
sand dollars or more in the previous year shall file a declaration of
estimated tax and remit one third of such amount on or before
June 1 , 1965 , provided that if any taxpayer uses the amount of
the tax liability for the previous calendar year as the estimate for
such declaration no penalties or interest shall apply to any sub-
sequent adjustments . The remainder of the tax shall be due on
or before March 1 , 1966 .
(2 ) As provided in subsection ( 1 ) of this section, but be-
ginning in 1966 , and each year thereafter , the tax due shall be
paid in three installments , one third on or before June 1, one

340 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
third on or before October 1 , and the remainder on or before
the following March 1. Provided , however , if any taxpayer uses
the amount of the tax liability for the previous calendar year as
the estimate for such declaration no penalties or interest shall
apply to any subsequent adjustments .
(3 ) Any adjustments may be made on or before October 1 .
All adjustments shall be made on or before March 1 .
Section 3. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
All such taxes not paid when due shall be subject to a penalty
of five per cent per month , but not more than twenty -five per
cent penalty shall be assessed on any one report , and interest at
the rate of one -half of one per cent per month from the date the
report was due .
Section 4. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
In the event any insurance company shall dissolve or retire
from this state , either voluntarily or involuntarily , during any
calendar year , such dissolution or retirement shall not defeat or
excuse the filing of reports and assessment and collection of
taxes imposed by this Act with respect to premiums written or
deposits held during that part of the calendar year prior to such
dissolution or retirement from this state .
Signed March 19, 1964

to be known as The Breaks Interstate Park, which shall be lo-

enson and Buchanan counties , Virginia . Said park shall be of

ARTICLE II .
There is hereby created The Breaks Interstate Park Com-
mission , which shall be a body corporate with the powers and
duties set forth herein and such additional powers as may be
conferred upon it by subsequent action of the appropriate au-
thorities of Kentucky and Virginia . The commission shall con-
sist of three commissioners from each of the two states , each of
whom shall be a citizen of the state he shall represent. Members
of the commission shall be appointed by the Governor . Va-
cancies shall be filled by the Governor for the unexpired term .
The term of one of the first commissioners appointed shall be for
two years, the term of another for three years , and the term of
the third for four years . Their successors shall be appointed
for terms of four years each. Each commissioner shall hold office
until his successor is appointed and qualified . An officer or
employe of the state , a political subdivision or the United States
government may be appointed a commissioner under this Act.
ARTICLE III .

strumentality of both the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the
Commonwealth of Virginia for the purpose of effecting the ob-

VEHICLE EQUIPMENT SAFETY COMPACT
ARTICLE
I
Findings and Purposes
(a) The party states find that
:
(
1) Accidents and deaths on their streets and highways
present
avery serious human and economic problem with
amajor
deleterious effect on the public welfare
.

CHAPTER 101 349
(2) There is a vital need for the development of greater
inter -jurisdictional cooperation to achieve the necessary uni-
formity in the laws , rules , regulations and codes relating to vehi-
cle equipment , and to accomplish this by such means as will
minimize the time between the development of demonstrably and
scientifically sound safety features and their incorporation into
vehicles .
(b) The purposes of this compact are to :
( 1 ) Promote uniformity in regulation of and standards for
equipment .
( 2) Secure uniformity of law and administrative practice
in vehicular regulations and related safety standards to permit
incorporation of desirable equipment changes in vehicles in the
interest of greater traffic safety .
(3 ) To provide means for the encouragement and utiliza-
tion of research which will facilitate the achievement of the fore-
going purposes , with due regard for the findings set forth in
subdivision ( a) of this Article .
( c) It is the intent of this compact to emphasize perform-
ance requirements and not to determine the specific detail of
engineering in the manufacture of vehicles or equipment except
to the extent necessary for the meeting of such performance re-
quirements .
ARTICLE II
Definitions
As used in this compact :
( a) "Vehicle " means every device in, upon or by which any
person or property is or may be transported or drawn upon a
highway , excepting devices moved by human power or used ex-
clusively upon stationary rails or tracks .
(b ) "State” means a state , territory or possession of the
United States , the District of Columbia , or the Commonwealth
of Puerto Rico .
( c) "Equipment " means any part of a vehicle or any acces-
sory for use thereon which affects the safety of operation of such
vehicle or the safety of the occupants .

350 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ARTICLE III
The Commission
( a) There is hereby created an agency of the party states
to be known as the "Vehicle Equipment Safety Commission "
hereinafter called the Commission . The Commission shall be
composed of one commissioner from each party state who shall
be appointed , serve and be subject to removal in accordance with
the laws of the state which he represents . If authorized by the
laws of his party state , a commissioner may provide for the dis-
charge of his duties and the performance of his functions on the
Commission , either for the duration of his membership or for
any lesser period of time, by an alternate . No such alternate
shall be entitled to serve unless notification of his identity and
appointment shall have been given to the Commission in such
form as the Commission may require . Each commissioner , and
each alternate , when serving in the place and stead of a com-
missioner , shall be entitled to be reimbursed by the Commission
for expenses actually incurred in attending Commission meetings
or while engaged in the business of the Commission .
(b) The commissioners shall be entitled to one vote each
on the Commission . No action of the Commission shall be bind-
ing unless taken at a meeting at which a majority of the total
number of votes on the Commission are cast in favor thereof .
Action of the Commission shall be only at a meeting at which a
majority of the commissioners , or their alternates , are present .
( c ) The Commission shall have a seal.

CHAPTER 101 357
ARTICLEX
Construction and Severability
This compact shall be liberally construed so as to effectuate
the purposes thereof . The provisions of this compact shall be
severable and if any phrase, clause, sentence or provision of this
compact is declared to be contrary to the Constitution of any
state or of the United States or the applicability thereof to any
government , agency, person or circumstance is held invalid , the
validity of the remainder of this compact and the applicability
thereof to any government , agency, person or circumstance shall
not be affected thereby . If this compact shall be held contrary
to the constitution of any state participating herein , the compact
shall remain in full force and effect as to the remaining party
states and in full force and effect as to the state affected as to all
severable matters .
Section 2. The legislature finds that :
1. The public safety necessitates the continuous develop-
ment, modernization and implementation of standards and re-
quirements of law relating to vehicle equipment , in accordance
with expert knowledge and opinion .
2. The public safety further requires that such standards
and requirements be uniform from jurisdiction to jurisdiction , ex-
cept to the extent that specific and compelling evidence supports
variation .
3. The Department of Public Safety, acting upon recom-
mendations of the Vehicle Equipment Safety Commission and
pursuant to the Vehicle Equipment Safety Compact provides a
just, equitable and orderly means of promoting the public safety
in the manner and within the scope contemplated by this act.
Section 3. The provisions of
allstatutes prescribing motor
vehicle equipment requirement shall continue
to

asrequired by this act

sion or provisions becomes effective pursuant to the Vehicle
Equipment Safety Compact and such other provisions of this act
as may be applicable .
Section 4. Pursuant to Article V ( e) of the Vehicle Equip-
ment Safety Compact , it is the intention of this state and it is
hereby provided that no rule , regulation or code issued by the
Vehicle Equipment Safety Commission in accordance with Arti-
cle V of the compact shall take effect until approved by act of
the legislature .
Section 5. The Commissioner of this state on the Vehicle
Equipment Safety Commission shall be the Commissioner of
Public Safety who shall serve during his continuance as such
officer . The commissioner of this state appointed pursuant to
this section may designate an alternate from among the officers
and employees of his agency to serve in his place and stead on
the Vehicle Equipment Safety Commission . Subject to the pro-
visions of the compact and bylaws of the Vehicle Equipment
Safety Commission , the authority and responsibilities of such
alternate shall be as determined by the Commissioner designating
such alternate .
Section 6. The Kentucky Employees Retirement System
may make an agreement with the Vehicle Equipment Safety
Commission for the coverage of said Commission's employees
pursuant to Article III ( f ) of the compact . Any such agreement,
as nearly as may be shall provide for arrangements similar to
those available to the employees of this state and shall be subject
to amendment or termination in accordance with its terms .
Section 7. Within appropriations available therefor , the
departments , agencies and officers of the government of
this state may cooperate with and assist the Vehicle Equipment
Safety Commission within the scope contemplated by Article
III ( h ) of the compact . The departments , agencies and officers
of the government of this state are authorized generally to co-
operate with said Commission .

CHAPTER 102

)
ACONCURRENT RESOLUTION relating to constitutional revision
.
WHEREAS

it
iswithin the power

toenact
proposals for not more than two amendments to the constitution
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky

WHEREAS

, generally speaking
, each regular session has
ordered two such amendments submitted to the electorate for rati-
fication or rejection at the following election
atwhich members
of
the General Assembly are elected
; and
WHEREAS
,
inresponse to the present need
tomodernize the constitu-
tion of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
, there
isnow sitting the
Constitution Revision Assembly
, same being an arm
ofLegislative
Research Commission as created by House Bill 39 of the 1964 Regular
Session
; and
WHEREAS
,
itcannot reliably be determined before sine die adjourn-
ment of the 1964 regular session
:

360 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
A. Whether the Constitution Revision Assembly will recommend guide-
lines to a constitutional convention to be called pursuant to Sections
258, 259 , 260, 261 , 262 , and 263 of the Constitution now in effect ,
or
B. Whether the Constitution Revision Assembly will recommend that
its proposed changes be made to the present constitution as an
amendment to the whole , or any part of parts thereof ; and
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of the Commonwealth of Kentucky,
the House of Representatives concurring therein :
First : It shall be the public policy of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky to encourage the Constitution Revision Assembly
to deliberate upon constitutional reform without hindrance and to
afford the assembly the unimpeded opportunity to recommend
whatever method it deems best to the end that the results of the
assembly will be submitted to the electorate for ratification or
rejection .
Second : In recognition of the possibility that the Constitu-
tion Revision Assembly may recommend that the 1964 session of
the General Assembly in called session propose revision of the
present constitution in the form of an amendment to the whole , or
to any part or parts thereof , it is declared to be the policy of the
1964 regular session of the General Assembly that no amendment
to the present constitution be proposed for submission at this
session of the General Assembly which adjourns sine die on
March 20 , 1964 .
Signed March 19, 1964

in other park property used, or to be used, for a zoo or zoological
gardens .

CHAPTER 109 365
Section 1. The county court clerk , circuit clerk , county
judge , sheriff , and jailer of each county shall receive an annual
salary of $9600 to be paid solely out of the statutory fees and
salaries received by him during the calendar year .
Section 2. KRS 64.345 is amended to read :
( 1 ) The circuit clerk , [county clerk ,] sheriff and jailer of
each county having a population of 75,000 or over shall receive
an annual salary of $ 9,600 .
(2) The amount , if any, allowed for the necessary office
expenses of each [such] officer shall be fixed by an order entered
upon the order book of the circuit court and county court, and
signed by the judges or a majority thereof of the [said ] courts .
A certified copy of the order , and of any subsequent changes made
therein , shall , as soon as entered, be forwarded to the Department
of Finance .
(3 ) Each [ such ] officer shall , on the first day of each
month , send to the Department of Finance a statement, sub-
scribed and sworn to by him, showing the amount of money
received or collected by or for him the preceding month , as fees
or compensation for official duties , and shall , with such statement ,
send to the Department of Finance the amount so collected or
received . The Department of Finance may extend the time for
filing the statement and making the payment for a period not
exceeding ten days in any month .
(4) The salary of each [such ] officer and his deputies and
assistants and his office expenses , shall be paid monthly by the
State Treasurer upon the warrant of the Department of Finance
made payable to the officer . If seventy-five percent of the
amount paid into the State Treasury in any month by any of
such officers is not sufficient to pay the salaries and expenses of
his office for that month , the deficit may be made up out of the
amount paid in any succeeding month ; but in no event shall the
amount allowed by the Department of Finance to any such offi-
cer for salaries and expenses exceed seventy-five percent of the
amount paid to the Department of Finance by the [ such ] officer
during his official term .
(5) The number of deputies and assistants allowed to each
[such ] officer and the compensation allowed to each deputy and

of any subsequent changes made therein , shall , as soon as entered ,
be forwarded to the Department of Finance .
Section 3. KRS 64.530 is amended to read:
( 1) Except as provided in subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of this
