CHAPTER 142 535
and but one branch office shall be open in any county at the same
time . Any person possessing the qualifications for registration
as a legal voter of any voting precinct in the county may appear
before the clerk during the period the books are open at the
office or branch office , and register or change his party affiliation
or transfer his registration card .
(3 ) For the purpose of registering voters in a city of the
second or third class which is not the county seat, the county
clerk shall maintain a branch office in the courthouse or city hall
of the city and take thereto the registration books for each voting
precinct in the city and keep them there during the periods the
books are open .
(4) Any voter who wishes to change his party affiliation
and who does not wish to apply in person may apply to the
county clerk , or the board of registration commissioners if he
resides in a city of the first class , by letter or by form which may
be provided . The clerk or board shall then mail a notice to the
address of the voter appearing on the existing registration records ,
stating that such application has been made and the particulars
thereof , and that the change will be made unless protest is filed at
the office of the clerk or the board within ten days from the date
of notice . If no protest is filed within the ten-day period , the
county clerk or the board shall change the party affiliation of the
voter as requested . If a protest is filed, the change shall not be
made until the clerk or the board has investigated the matter
and given the protesting voter an opportunity to be heard . The
date on which the change is made shall be noted on the voter's
registration record . No change of party affiliation shall be made
except on those days when registrations are allowed under sub-
section ( 1 ) of this section .
Section 3. Section 118.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Certificates of nomination issued by the State Board of
Elections [ Commissioners ] shall be filed by that board with
the Secretary of State immediately . Such certificates issued by
the county election commission shall be filed by that commission
with the county clerk immediately .
( 2 ) Certificates of nomination made by the governing au

536 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
thority of a party to fill vacancies in office , as provided in KRS
119.030 , shall, when required to be filed with the Secretary of
State , be filed not less than fifty -five days before the day fixed
by law for the election of the person in nomination . When re-
quired to be filed with the county court clerk , they shall be filed
not less than forty -five days before the day fixed by law for the
election of the person in nomination .
(3 ) Except as provided in subsection (4) of this section ,
petitions and certificates of nomination made under KRS 118.080
and 118.090 , when required to be filed with the Secretary of State ,
shall be filed not less than fifty -five days before the day fixed by
law for the holding of primary elections . When required to be
filed with the county court clerk, they shall be filed not less than
forty -five days before the day fixed by law for holding of primary
elections .
(4) Petitions and certificates of nomination made under
KRS 118.080 and 118.090 for the nomination of candidates for city
offices or of candidates for members of boards of education shall
be filed not less than forty-five nor more than sixty days before
the day fixed by law for the election of the person in nomination ,
except that where any independent school district or independent
graded school district comprises parts of two or more counties the
petitions or certificates shall be filed with the Secretary of State
not less than fifty -five days prior to the day fixed for the election
of the person in nomination . Certificates of nomination for elec-
tors of President and Vice President of the United States shall be
filed with the Secretary of State not less than fifty -five days prior
to the date fixed by law for the election of such electors .
Section 4. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
No candidate's name shall appear on any ballot more than
once except as provided in KRS 119.070 .
Section 5. Section 118.240 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The county executive committee of any political party
having a ticket to elect at any regular election , may designate
not more than two challengers to be present at and witness the

CHAPTER 142 537
holding of the election in each precinct in the county . The
challengers shall be entitled to stay in the room or at the door .
The challengers shall be appointed in writing signed by the chair-
man of the committee and shall produce written appointments
on demand of any election officer .
(2) Any school board candidate , independent candidate , or
independent ticket at the regular election may designate not more
than one challenger to be present at and witness the holding of
the elections in each precinct in the county . The challenger shall
be entitled to stay in the room or at the door . The challenger
shall be appointed in writing by the chairman of the committee,
independent candidate or candidates representing a ticket , and
shall produce written appointment on demand of any election
officer .
( 3 ) Each challenger shall take the following oath: "You do
solemnly swear that you will faithfully and impartially discharge
the duties as official challenger assigned by law; that you will
not cause any delay to persons offering to vote , further than is
necessary to procure satisfactory information of the qualification
of such person as an elector ; that you will use no means to in-
fluence a voter ; and that you will not disclose to any person how
any elector has voted ."
Section 6. Section 118.380 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
In all regular elections , the governing authority of each
political party , each candidate for member of board of education ,
independent candidate or independent ticket may designate to
the county board of election commissioners one representative to
meet with the board as provided in KRS 118.370 . The board shall
permit such representatives to assist in and witness the count of
the ballots in all the precincts in the county , and each of such
representatives shall have equal and full opportunity to inspect
and count the ballots .

CHAPTER 143
(Н. В. 46)
AN ACT relating to Fish and Wildlife Resources .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 150.330 is amended to read:
(1) No person shall take any wild birds except during the
open season prescribed by the department for the particular
species .
[The open season for quail may be between November 1 ,
and February 1 only, and shall not exceed a total of sixty days ,
consecutive or otherwise . The open season for ruffed grouse or
native pheasant may be between November 1 and February 1
only, but shall not exceed a total of sixty days, consecutive or
otherwise .]
There shall be no open season for wild turkey , exotic pheasants or
chukar partridge until the commissioner , with the approval of the
commission , finds the conditions and population warrant it. Then
regulations shall be enacted to govern same . No person shall take
quail , ruffed grouse or native pheasant except from sunrise to
sunset .
(2) No person shall take , pursue , possess , transport , purchase
or sell or attempt to do so, any migratory birds , except as author-
ized by the Migratory Bird Treaty Act (40 Stat. 755) and regula-
tions under it.

542 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3) No person over sixteen years of age shall hunt any wild
ducks , geese or brant unless, at the time , in addition to the
appropriate state hunting license , he has on his person a valid
Migratory Bird Hunting Stamp of current issue as required by the
Migratory Bird Hunting Stamp Act (48 Stat. 451) as amended.
Section 2. KRS 150.370 is amended to read:
(1) No person shall take any wild animal protected by this
chapter except during the open season for the particular species
as prescribed by the department . [The open season for rabbits
may be between November 1 and February 1 only , but shall not
exceed a total of sixty days , consecutive or otherwise . The open
season for mink , opossum , racoon , skunk , muskrat and red fox may
be between November 1 and February 15 only, but shall not
exceed a total of sixty days , consecutive or otherwise , except that
the department may allow an additional thirty days in which
opossum and racoon may be hunted and taken by dog only . The
open season for squirrel may be between August 1 and January
1 only , but shall not exceed a total of ninety days , consecutive or
otherwise . ] The open season for pond , bull or jumbo frogs shall
be such period as the department may prescribe . There shall be
no open season for otter or beaver .
(2) Subsection (1) of this section shall not apply to persons
who hunt red fox at night with dogs for sport and not to kill; but
this exception in favor of sportsmen shall not extend to anyone
engaged in the business of a fur dealer or a trapper or a profes-
sional hunter .
(3) The possession of any wild animals, fur bearers , or the
raw fur thereof , when the season is not open for the particular
species shall be prima facie evidence that the animal was taken
out of open season , but this presumption shall not apply to pos-
session of any such animal or fur by a trapper during the open
season as prescribed by the department and fifteen days there-
after , or by a fur buyer during the open season and twenty days
thereafter .
Signed March 21, 1964

CHAPTER 144
(Н. В. 99 )
AN ACT relating to service contracts between political subdivision .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. KRS 79.110 is amended to read :
Notwithstanding any existing means provided by law for
joint [city-county ] functions by any political subdivisions of this
state , the governing bodies of any two or more political subdivi-
sions within the same county , which may include the fiscal court ,
[the fiscal court of any county and the governing body of any
city within that county ] may enter into contractual arrangements
with each other for the performance of governmental services
anywhere within the boundaries of the county [ , both within and
without the boundary of the city] .
shall:
Section 2. KRS 79.120 is amended to read :
Any contractual arrangement entered into under KRS 79.110
( 1) Name the service or services to be performed and
clearly define same ;
(2 ) State which subdivision of government shall render
the service or services ;
( 3 ) Define the area within which the service or services
contracted for shall be performed ;
(4) Provide the means of payment of the cost of the serv-
ice or services ;
( 5 ) When the county government is one of the contracting
parties, take cognizance of the fact that city residents are county
residents and taxpayers to both governments , and provide for an
equitable distribution of the cost of providing the service or
services .
Section 3. KRS 79.140 is amended to read:
The governing body of any other political subdivision [city]
within the boundaries of the county , which may include the fiscal

544 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
court , may , with the mutual consent of [the fiscal court and ]
the governing body of each political subdivision [city] constitut-
ing a party thereto , become a party to and beneficiary of a con-
tract entered into under authority of KRS 79.110 . The inclusion
of a third -party political subdivision [city ] in any such contract
shall be by a writing in compliance with KRS 79.120 .
Section 4. KRS 79.150 is amended to read :
( 1 ) The governing body of any city electing to render or
to receive governmental services under a contract authorized by
KRS 79.110 to 79.180 may , by ordinance , abolish any office ren-
dered unnecessary thereby , or may, by ordinance , declare any
such office vacant during the term of the contract . Any such
office occupied by an official elected by the qualified voters of
the city shall not be abolished or declared vacant before the end
of the term of the incumbent .
(2) The fiscal court or the appointing authority of any
county or other political subdivision electing to render or to
receive governmental services under a contract authorized by
KRS 79.110 to 79.180 may declare vacant for the term of the
contract any office rendered unnecessary thereby . Any such
office occupied by an officer elected by the qualified voters of
the county or other political subdivision shall not be declared
vacant before the end of the term of the incumbent .
Section 5. KRS 79.160 is amended to read:
( 1 ) When the governing bodies of any two or more political
subdivisions within the same county , which may include the fiscal
court , [when the fiscal court of any county and the governing
body of any city within that county ] enter into a contract for
the rendering of governmental services [to the county by the city
or to the city by the county ] , the contract shall make such
provision for the transfer of employes from the departments of
the one or more subdivision or subdivisions of government receiv-
ing the service to the departments of the subdivision of govern-
ment rendering the service as may effect the efficient functioning
of the plan for service to the people .
(2 ) In the event that in accordance with the provisions of
subsection ( 1 ) of this section , employes are transferred to a

CHAPTER 145 545
governmental department subject to any civil service laws or
regulation , those transferred employes shall during the term of
the contract be subject to any such civil service laws or regula-
tions . Such employes , when transferred , shall be considered as
having satisfied all of the qualifications for obtaining original
appointment under applicable civil service laws and regulations ,
and shall be considered as having been given probationary ap-
pointments as defined in such laws or regulations as of the date
they are so placed in the classified service .
Section 6. KRS 79.170 is amended to read:
Any contract entered into under the provisions of KRS 79.110
to 79.180 shall be for a term of at least two years and not more
than four years . At least one year before the termination date of
any such contract , each contracting party shall in writing notify
the other or others of its intention to enter into a new contract
concerning the services rendered and received or of
itsintention
toterminate the arrangements
.
Signed March 21
, 1964

CHAPTER 146

AN ACT relating to Housing Projects .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. KRS 80.010 is amended to read :
As used in this chapter , unless the context otherwise requires :
( 1 ) "Housing project " means a building or buildings con-
taining rooms to be provided as living quarters , together with
shops , stores , garages , laundries , doctors' and dentists' offices and
other facilities and appurtenances deemed reasonably necessary
by the housing commission to the successful and economical
operation of the project . It also means any work or undertaking
of a housing commission or of the Federal Government to :
( a) Demolish , clear or remove a building or buildings from
any slum area , including the adaptation of such area to recrea-

( b ) Provide decent , safe and sanitary living accommoda-

as determined by the commission undertaking the project , to
enable them , without financial assistance , to obtain such accom-
modations ; such work or undertaking may include buildings , land
equipment , facilities and other real or personal property for
necessary , convenient or desirable appurtenances , streets , sewers ,
water service , parks , site preparation , gardening , administrative ,
community , health , recreational , welfare or other purposes ;
( c) Accomplish a combination of the foregoing .
( 2) "Housing commission " or " commission" means any
housing commission created pursuant to this chapter .
( 3) "Public body" means any city, village , county , com-
mission , district , authority or other public body or political sub-

CHAPTER 148
(Н. В. 153)
AN ACT relating to motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. KRS 186.060 is amended to read :
(1 ) Applications for registration of motor vehicles owned
exclusively by a county , city or board of education in the state
or by the state or Federal government shall be accompanied by a
statement from the head of the department of the governmental
unit that owns the motor vehicle , certifying that the motor vehicle
is exclusively owned and operated by the governmental unit . The
application and statement shall be forwarded by the county clerk
to the department , which shall give special authority to the clerk
to register it . Upon receiving that authority the clerk shall issue
a registration receipt and the official number plate described in
KRS 186.240 ( 3 ) , and report the registration to the head of the
department authorizing the registration . For his services in issu-
ing such certificate of registration and number plate and
reporting the same , the county clerk shall be entitled to a fee of
fifty cents in each instance , to be paid by the department upon
whose authorization such license was issued .
(2 ) After such registration of any vehicle owned exclusively
by a county , city or board of education in the state or by the state

552 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or federal government [ the state ] and after issuance of such
number plate for such vehicle so owned , no subsequent regis-
tration or renewal of same , and no subsequent renewal of a num-
ber plate of said vehicle shall be necessary so long as said vehicle
is owned exclusively by such governmental unit [the state , ]
except in the case of loss or destruction of said license plate . In
the event of such loss or destruction , said number plate shall be
replaced in the same manner as if no plate had ever been issued .
(3) When a motor vehicle owned exclusively by a county ,
city or board of education in the state or by the state or federal
government is transferred or sold to another governmental unit
a new license plate shall be issued for the vehicle in the same
manner as provided for in subsection ( 1 ) of this section and shall
have the same effect as given to such license plates in subsection
(2) of this section .
(4) [ ( 3 ) ] No person shall use on a motor vehicle , not ex-
clusively owned by a county , city board of education in the state ,
or the state or federal government , any license plate that has
been issued for use on a motor vehicle owned by such a govern-
mental unit.
Signed March 21, 1964
CHAPTER 149
(Н. В. 164)
AN ACT relating to General Corporations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. KRS 271.055 is amended to read:
( 1 ) The articles of incorporation in triplicate originals shall
be acknowledged by at least [three] one of the incorporators
before an officer authorized by the laws of this state to take
acknowledgments and the triplicate originals shall be delivered
to the Secretary of State . If he finds that the articles of incorpora-
tion have been signed and acknowledged according to law, he

CHAPTER 149 553

shall, when all taxes, fees and charges have been paid as required by law, record one original in his office, and issue a certificate of incorporation. There shall be paid to the Secretary of State a fee of $3.00 for recording the articles, and a fee of $1.00 for issuing a certificate of incorporation.

(2) The certificate of incorporation together with the remaining original articles of incorporation, endorsed with the fact and time of recording in the office of the Secretary of State, shall be returned to the *incorporator or incorporators or his or their* representatives. One original shall then be filed with and recorded by the clerk of the county court of the county in which the registered office of the corporation is situated, and the other shall be retained by the corporation. The clerk of the county court shall be paid a fee of $3.00 for recording said articles.

(3) No foreign corporation, except a foreign insurance company, shall be authorized to do business in this state until it has filed a certified copy of its articles of incorporation and all existing amendments in the office of the Secretary of State. It shall thereafter file a certified copy of subsequent amendments to its articles in the office of the Secretary of State. Such corporations shall pay to the Secretary of State an original filing fee of $25.00 and a recording fee of $10.00 for recording the articles of incorporation and accompanying amendments, and shall pay the same recording fee for recording subsequent amendments.

(4) A copy of any articles of incorporation and all amendments thereto of both domestic and foreign corporations as recorded in the office of the Secretary of State, under the certificate and seal of that office, shall be accepted as evidence in any court for any purpose in any action in which the original could be used. For furnishing a certified copy of the record to any party, the Secretary of State shall charge the same fee to be charged for the recordation of that instrument.

CHAPTER 154
(Н. В. 249 )
AN ACT relating to the practice of clinical psychology .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 319.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
[The practice of clinical psychology is defined as follows : ]
( 1 ) A person practices psychology within the meaning
of this chapter when he renders to individuals , groups , organiza-
tions , or the public any service involving the application except as
specific applications are excluded in Section 15 of this Act,
of principles , methods or procedures of the science and pro-
fession of psychology , to include :
( a) evaluation of: disorders of cognitive processes and emo-
tions , and problems of adjustment in individuals or in groups ,
through interpretation of tests of mental abilities , aptitudes , inter-
ests , attitudes , emotions, motivation , and personality characteris-
tics ;
(b) recommendation for : selection of personnel , arrange-
ment of effective work and learning situations , resolution of
interpersonal or social conflicts , and change of employment status ,
including termination of employment when based on interpreta-
tion of tests of the types noted in sub -section (a) above;
(c) application of: principles and methods of psychological
modification of behavior , including hypnosis , and the results of
research on such modification of behavior , to individuals or to
groups .
This definition does not include the teaching of principles
of psychology for accredited educational institutions , or the con-
duct of research in problems of human behavior .
[who ( a ) renders to individual clients for fees or personal
profit , any professional service requiring the application of recog-
nized principles , methods and procedures of the science and pro-
fession of clinical psychology , such as the administration and
interpretation of standardized tests of mental abilities and per-

classification or evaluation ; or, ( b ) for fees or personal profit,
applies such psychological techniques for purposes of re-
education , guidance or readjustment .]
Nothing in this definition shall be construed as permitting
the administration or prescribing of drugs or in any way infring-
ing upon the practice of medicine as defined in the laws of this
state.

AN ACT relating to the probation of wills .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
KRS 394.140 is amended to read as follows :
Wills shall be proved before , and admitted to record by ,
the county court of the testator's residence (if such residence
was on a United States army post , military reservation or fort ,
it may be proved and admitted to record in the county court
of any county adjacent thereto); if he had no known place of
residence in this state , and land is devised , then in the county
where the land or part thereof lies ; if no land is devised , then
in the county where he died , or where his estate or part thereof
is , or where there is a debt or demand owing to him .

CHAPTER 158
(Н. В. 271)
AN ACT relating to the protection of forests and to forest fire control .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky declares as a public policy of the Commonwealth the
prevention and control of forest fires on or threatening the forest
land within the Commonwealth in order to preserve forest and
other natural resources , enhance the growth and maintenance of
forests , conserve forest cover on watersheds , protect recreational ,
wildlife and other values , promote stability of forest -using indus-
tries , and prevent loss of life and damage to property from wild-
fires and other conflagrations .

578 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 2. As used in this Act, unless the context requires
otherwise :
estry .
( 1 ) "Director " means the Director of the Division of For-
( 2) "Division" means the Division of Forestry .
(3 ) "Person " means an individual , corporation , partnership ,
association , municipality , state and federal government , or other
public body or other legal entity , or any officer , employee or
agent of any of the foregoing .
( 4) "Timberland " means any land which has enough timber
or woody brush , standing or down , to constitute a fire menace to
itself or adjoining lands , but does not include lands under culti-
vation or entirely in grass , nor land that is an isolated fire risk
unless a fire on it would imperil the lands of an adjoining land-
owner .
(5 ) “ Flammable material ” shall include but is not limited to
refuse , debris , waste forest material , brush , stumps , logs , rubbish ,
fallen timber , grass , stubble , leaves , slash , and grain .
Section 3. No person shall :
( 1) Dispose of a lighted match , cigarette , cigar , ashes , or
other flaming or glowing substance, or any other substance or
thing in such condition that it is likely to ignite a forest , brush ,
grass or woods fire , or throw or drop any of the aforesaid objects
or substances from a moving vehicle or drop or leave any of the
aforesaid objects or substances within the limits of the right-of-
way of any road or highway .
( 2) Smoke in forest land during any emergency period of
fire danger as set by the Division of Forestry , except as may be
permitted by rules and regulations of such agency.
(3) Set fire or cause or procure the setting on fire of any
flammable material on forest land of another without the consent
of the owner .
(4) Set fire or cause or procure the setting on fire of any
flammable material on forest land , without notifying adjacent
landowners and without taking reasonable precautions both be

CHAPTER 159
579

fore and after lighting the fire and at all times thereafter to prevent escape thereof.

(5) Deposit or leave unattended on any railroad or highway right-of-way any fire or live coals, or set or maintain thereon any fire for the purpose of cooking, heating, or providing light or warmth unless such fire is enclosed within a stove, drum, fireplace or other properly prepared location.

(6) Set a backfire or cause a backfire to be set, except under the direct supervision of the Division of Forestry, or unless it can be established that the setting of such backfire is necessary for the purpose of saving life or valuable property.

(7) Without authority, destroy, deface, or remove any notice, sign, or poster of the Division of Forestry, posted for the better protection of wood lots, forests or wild lands from fire or trespass.

(8) Use or operate on or adjacent to forest land, a welding torch, tarpot, or other device which may cause a fire, without clearing flammable material surrounding the operation or without taking such other reasonable precautions necessary to ensure against the starting and spreading of fire.

(9) Light, maintain or use a fire for cooking, heating or providing light or warmth upon lands not his own without first obtaining permission from the owner, lessee or agent thereof.

(10) Light or maintain on or adjacent to forest land, a bonfire within one hundred fifty feet (150 feet), of any house or building.

(11) Discharge or cause to be discharged a gun firing incendiary or tracer bullets or tracer charge or combustible gun wadding onto or across any forest land.

(12) Have in his possession on forest land any incendiary or tracer bullet or tracer charge, except in the course of transporting the same in conformity with law.

(13) Light or maintain on or adjacent to forest land an open fire or campfire without clearing flammable material surrounding such fire as necessary to ensure against the escape or spread thereof, or leave such fire before it is extinguished.

Section 10. ( 1 ) It shall be unlawful, when the forest lands ,
brush lands and fields in this state or any part thereof have be-
come so dry or parched as to create an extraordinary fire hazard
endangering lives and property , for any person, except the owner ,
tenant or owner's authorized agent , persons regularly engaged in
cutting , processing , or moving forest products , persons engaged
in constructing , maintaining , and operating utility of pipeline
rights - of- way or persons on official duty, to enter or travel in
any state , county , municipal or private forest lands , brush lands ,
fields or idle or abandoned lands in the area so affected except
on public highways or well defined private roads .
(2 ) When such an emergency is found to exist by the Di-
rector of Forestry , the Governor may proclaim such a condition
to exist in the Commonwealth or any described part thereof . The
provisions of subsection ( 1 ) shall be effective only during the time
such proclamation is in force and only in the area where such
emergency is declared to exist .
( 3 ) The Secretary of State shall cause notice of such procla-
mation or any amendments or rescission thereof to be published
in a newspaper or newspapers of general circulation in the area
affected .
Section 11. The Commonwealth Attorney or County Attor-
ney shall initiate and prosecute appropriate abatement proceed-
ings by injunction or otherwise , for the prevention or correction
of any condition constituting or threatening to constitute a vio-
lation of this Act. The institution or pendancy of a proceeding
pursuant to this section shall not bar the imposition of any penal-
ties or the securing of any other relief provided by this Act , or
rules or regulations promulgated thereunder .
Section 12. The Division of Forestry shall administer this
Act and shall have power to issue , amend and revise such rules
and regulations as may be authorized hereby or as may reasonably
be necessary to implement the same . The division is authorized
to make , conduct or participate in any investigations and surveys
designed to establish the cause of and responsibility for a particu-

impair the jurisdiction or powers of any other department , agency
or officer of or in this state to investigate, apprehend , prosecute
or punish violations of law.
Section 13. In connection with the administration of this
Act and any rule or regulation pursuant hereto , any duly author-

enforce any provisions of this Act shall have power to enter upon

Section 18. ( 1 ) Any person who violates any of the pro-
visions of this Act or any regulation adopted hereunder , for which
no definite fine or imprisonment is fixed by this section, shall be
fined not more than two hundred dollars or imprisoned for not
more than three months , or both such fine and imprisonment .
(2) Any person who violates any of the provisions of Sec-
tion 5 shall be fined not more than one thousand dollars and im-
prisoned for not more than two years , or both such fine and im-
prisonment .
(3) Any person who violates Section 3(7) shall be fined
not less than ten nor more than fifty dollars or imprisoned for not
less than ten days nor more than six months, or both such fine
and imprisonment .
(4) Any person who violates any provision of Section 4 or
Section 9 shall be fined not less than ten nor more than one hun-
dred dollars or imprisoned for not less than ten days or more
than six months , or both such fine and imprisonment , for each
separate offense .
(5) Any person who violates any provision of Section 10
shall be fined not less than ten nor more than five hundred dol-
lars , or imprisoned for not more than thirty days , or both such
fine and imprisonment .

CHAPTER 161

AN ACT relating to city sidewalks and fire hydrants .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. KRS 107.020 is amended to read :
( 1 ) The term " governing body," as used in this chapter ,
means and includes the legislative body of any city , whether the
same be designated by applicable statutes as a general council , a
common council , a city council , a board of trustees , a board of
commissioners , or otherwise .
(2) The term " ordinance " means and includes any
ordinance enacted in accordance with the general laws applicable
to ordinances of the class of city in question , and the form of
government thereof , and in accordance with the provisions of
this chapter .
( 3) The term “public way ” means and includes streets ,
boulevards , avenues , roads , lanes , alleys , parkways , courts , ter-
races , and other courses of travel open to the general public by
whatsoever name designated .
(4) The terms “improvement " and "project " mean and
include ( a ) the new construction of public ways or the sub-
stantial reconstruction or widening thereof , ( b ) the construction ,
installation or substantial reconstruction of sanitary, storm , or
combined sewers and appurtenances , ( c ) the new construction ,
enlargement or substantial reconstruction of sewage treatment
plants for rendering sewage less hazardous to public health , safety
and general welfare , [or ] (e) the construction , installation or
substantial replacement of fire hydrants in cities of the 3rd
thru 6th classes , necessary water mains and appurtenances
in a city in a county containing a city of the first class
or (f) any combination of the same . Bonds for improvements
defined in ( b ) and ( c ) of this subsection may be caused to
mature as to principal in term or serial maturities not to exceed
thirty years from date of issue , instead of being limited to twenty
years from date of issue as otherwise provided in KRS 107.090 .
( 5) The term "costs " as applied to any project undertaken

under this chapter includes the cost of labor , materials , and equip-
ment necessary to complete the project in a satisfactory manner ,
cost of land acquired , and every expense connected with the
project , including preliminary and other surveys , inspections of
the work, engineers' fees and costs , attorneys' fees, preparation
of plans and specifications , publication of ordinances and notices ,
interest which will accrue on the bonds until the due date of
the first annual improvement assessment levied in connection
therewith , a sum equal to any discount in the sale of the bonds ( if
discount bids are authorized and permitted by the governing
body ) , a reasonable allowance for unforeseen contingencies , the
printing of bonds , and other costs of financing which may include
the payment of a fee to a fiscal agent for advice and assistance
in the preparation and marketing of the bonds .
(6) The term "assessed value basis" means the plan for
the levying of annual improvement benefit assessments on the
basis of the assessed values of the benefited properties , as author-
ized by this chapter .
(7) The term "front-foot basis " refers to the plan for
financing improvements by apportioning the cost among benefited
properties upon the basis of the number of linear feet thereof
abutting upon the improvement project , as otherwise provided
by law.

CHAPTER 162
(Н. В. 314
)
AN ACT relating to trespass on property
.
Be
itenacted
bythe General Assembly
ofthe Commonwealth
ofKentucky
:

CHAPTER 163 593
Section 1. KRS 433.720 is amended to read:
(1) Any person who, without the consent of the owner ,
enters any orchard , game preserve , ginseng garden , [farm ] or
other premises enclosed by a fence not less than seven feet in
height , after the owner has conspicuously displayed on the
premises on a board not less than twelve by twenty -four inches
in size the word "posted"; and any person who damages any
such fence enclosing such premises shall be confined in the
penitentiary for not less than one nor more than three years .
(2) Any person who , without the consent of the owner
willfully enters or goes upon the enclosed farm premises of
another , whereon the owner , or other person in charge of the
premises has previously posted plainly written signs or warn-
ings thereon , where they may be readily seen , no more
than four hundred yards apart along or near the boundaries
of the premises, using the words " no trespassing," "keep out"
or "posted " or words of similar meaning or any person who
molests , injures , removes or destroys any such posted sign or
warning by the owner or person in charge of the premises
shall be punished by a fine of not more than five hundred dollars ,
or by confinement in the county jail for not more than six months ,
or both .
Signed March 21 , 1964

CHAPTER 165
(Н. В. 367)
AN ACT relating to definitions and the establishment of the Women's
Prison as a separate institution .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 197.010 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Definitions as used in this chapter , unless the context other-
wise require :
( 1 ) "State Agency ” means any department , board , com-
mission or agency of the state government ;
( 2 ) "Department " means Department of Corrections ;
( 3 ) "Penitentiaries " includes the state penal institutions for
males at Eddyville and LaGrange , the institution for Women
located in Shelby County , together with the branches thereof
[in Shelby and Franklin Counties ] and any other similar institu-
tions hereafter established .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The institution for women shall be known as " The
Kentucky Correctional Institutional for Women . "
Signed March 21 , 1964
CHAPTER 166
(Н. В. 368 )
AN ACT relating to employment of prisoners outside the walls of the
institution .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 197.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :

CHAPTER 167 599
No prisoner who [has been sentenced for ] is serving a
sentence for rape , attempted rape , detaining a woman against
her will , or who has escaped or attempted to escape from an adult
correctional institution within the past five years , [ or who has
been convicted and sentenced more than twice ] , [ or who has
been convicted of highway robbery or bank robbery ] , or who
has been convicted of armed robbery or armed assault with intent
to rob where any injury has been inflicted upon any other person
during the commission of the offense , shall be worked or released
for work outside of the walls of the prison . No prisoner who has
been sentenced to life imprisonment shall be worked or released
for work outside of the walls of the prison until he has actually
served within the walls of the prison for at least one year of his
sentence .
Signed March 21 , 1964

if he has been discharged for misconduct connected with his
most recent work, but legitimate activity in connection with labor
organizations or failure to join a company union shall not be

(2 ) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits
or serving a waiting period for the duration of any period of un-
employment with respect to which :
( a ) He was discharged for dishonesty in connection with
his most recent work ;
(b ) He voluntarily quit his most recent work to marry or

or
( c ) He has left his most recent suitable work voluntarily
without good cause .
( 3 ) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits
for any week with respect to which he knowingly made a false
statement to establish his right to or the amount of his benefits ;
and , within the succeeding twenty -four months , for such addi-
tional weeks immediately following the date of discovery [such
determination ] , not to exceed a total of fifty -two , as may be de-
termined by the Commissioner .
(4) No worker shall be disqualified under paragraph (b )
of subsection ( 1 ) or subsection ( 2 ) of this section unless the
employer , within a reasonable time as prescribed by commission
regulation , notifies the Division and the worker in writing of
the alleged voluntary quitting or the discharge for misconduct ;

CHAPTER 168 607

voluntary quitting or discharge for misconduct if such alleged
voluntary quitting or discharge for misconduct is known to the
Commissioner prior to the time benefits are paid to such worker ;
provided further , that the exercise of such right by the Com-
missioner , in the absence of timely notice from the employer , shall
not relieve the employer's reserve account of benefit charges
under the provisions of KRS 341.530 ( 3 ) .
( 5 ) As used in this section and in subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
341.530 , " most recent ” work shall be construed as that work which
occurred after the first day of the worker's base period and which
last preceded the week of unemployment with respect to which
benefits are claimed ; except that , if the work last preceding such
week of unemployment was intermittent or temporary in na-
ture , most recent work may be construed as that work last pre-
ceding such intermittent or temporary work.

CHAPTER 169
(Н. В. 403 )
AN ACT relating to the "friend of the court ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. KRS 403.090 is amended to read:
( 1 ) The fiscal court of any county may , by resolution , au-
thorize the appointment of a "friend of the court " . If the circuit
court of the county has but one judge , the appointment shall be
made by the judge . If the court has two or more judges , the
appointment shall be made by joint action of the judges , at the
general term . The appointment shall be for a term of two years ,
subject to removal at the pleasure of the appointing authority .
Vacancies shall be filled by appointment of the appoint-
ing authority for the unexpired term . The person appointed shall
be a licensed practicing attorney . He shall take the Constitutional
oath of office and shall give bond in such sum as may be fixed
by the appointing judge or judges .
( 2) It shall be the duty of the friend of the court to super-
vise and enforce the payment of sums ordered or adjudged by
the circuit court in divorce actions to be paid for the care and

CHAPTER 169 611
maintenance of minor children . All persons who have been or-
dered or adjudged by the court , in connection with divorce ac-
tions , to make payments for the care and maintenance of children ,
shall , if so ordered by the court , make such payments to the
friend of the court , and he shall see that the payments are prop-
erly applied in accordance with the order or judgment . However ,
if the court so directs , the payments may be made through the
juvenile court of the county ; in such case the friend of the court
shall render such assistance as may be required in keeping rec-
ords concerning such payments and in the enforcement of delin-
quent payments , and the circuit court may direct that a desig-
nated amount or portion of the funds appropriated by the fiscal
court for expenses of the friend of the court be paid to the juve-
nile court as reimbursement for the expenses incurred by the
juvenile court in connection with the handling of such payments .
The friend of the court shall promptly investigate
allcases where

ofchildren for
whom payments are being made

tothe court
all
cases
inwhich the children are not receiving proper care
ormain-
tenance
,
or
inwhich the person having custody
isfailing
tofur-
nish proper custody
. He shall make such other reports
tothe
court
asthe court may require
.

Inany action for divorce where the parties have minor
children
, the friend
ofthe court
,
ifrequested by the trial judge
,
shall make such investigation
aswill enable him
toascertain
all
facts and circumstances that will affect the rights and interests
ofthe children and will enable the court
toenter just and proper
orders and judgment concerning the care
, custody and main-
tenance
ofthe children
. He shall make
areport
tothe trial judge
,
at
atime fixed by the judge
,setting forth his recommendations
as
tothe care
, custody and maintenance
ofthe children
. He may re-
quest the court
topostpone the final submission
ofany case
to

areasonable time
inwhich
tocomplete his investiga-
tion

(4) The friend
ofthe court shall have authority
tosecure
the issuance by the court
ofany order
, rule
orcitation necessary

or refuse to renew or issue a license for the reason therein stated ,
that the licensee or applicant has , in the judgment of the com-
mission, been guilty of an act detrimental to the interests of
boxing or wrestling generally or to the public interest, conven-
ience or necessity , including , but not by way of limitation , the
violation of any of the provisions of this chapter or any rule or
regulation of the commission .
(2) Without limiting the discretion of the commission as
provided in this chapter , the commission may suspend or revoke
a license or refuse to renew or issue a license , if it shall find that

jurisdiction or any rule , regulation or order of the commission ,
or shall have violated any rule of boxing or wrestling which shall
have been approved or adopted by the commission , or has been
guilty of or engaged in similar , related or like practices .

of the bond , the commission shall issue to the applicant a certi-
ficate of filing and approval , which shall be filed by the applicant
with the commission , with the application for the permit . No such
permit shall be issued until such certificate has been filed.

**Section 17.** No boxing or wrestling match or exhibition shall be of more than twelve rounds in length except championship matches, which shall be limited in the discretion of the commission.

**Section 18.** Contestants in a professional or amateur boxing or wrestling match or exhibition shall be examined by a reputable licensed physician appointed by the commission, and shall be in excellent health before participating in such a boxing or wrestling match or exhibition.

**Section 19.** Decisions may be rendered in any boxing or wrestling match or exhibition, permitted by this chapter, in the discretion of the commission and by such method as it by rule prescribes.

**Section 20.** All structures or parts of structures used, or intended to be used, for boxing and wrestling matches and exhibitions, shall be properly ventilated and provided with fire exits and fire escapes, if need be, and shall conform to the laws, ordinances and regulations pertaining to buildings in the city where situated.

**Section 21.** No contestant under eighteen years of age shall be knowingly issued a license or allowed to participate in any professional boxing or wrestling match or exhibition, nor shall any person obtain, or cause to be obtained, a license to any person under eighteen years of age, to participate in any professional boxing or wrestling match or exhibition.

**Section 22.** All peace officers, on being informed or having reason to believe that a professional match that is not authorized under this chapter is about to take place, or that there is training or preparation for such a contest, in any place within their jurisdiction, shall suppress and prevent it.

For this purpose any peace officer may enter any place where such contest is being held or will be held or where there is training or preparation for such a contest and may arrest without a warrant any person who does not submit satisfactory proof that he has whatever license or permit is required by this chapter.

CHAPTER 173 629
CHAPTER 172
(Н. В. 422 )
AN ACT relating to property taxes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Personal property of non -residents of the state
in their original package or fungible goods in bulk , belonging to
a non-resident of the state , shipped into this state and placed in
a public warehouse for the purpose of trans-shipment to an out-
of-state or within -the- state destination and so designated on the
original bill of lading , or personal property of residents of the
state in their original package and fungible goods in bulk , belong-
ing to a resident of the state , placed in a public warehouse for
the purpose of trans-shipment to an out -of-state destination and
so designated on the original bill of lading , shall , while so in the
original package , or as fungible goods in bulk, in such warehouse ,
be subject to an annual ad valorem tax to the state equal to five
cents upon each one hundred dollars of the fair cash value of such
personal property . No portion of a premises owned or leased by
a consignor or consignee , or a subsidiary of a consignor or con-
signee , shall be deemed to be a public warehouse within the
meaning of this Act despite any licensing as such .
Section 2. No other tax shall be assessed by the state or
any county , city or other taxing district on such personal prop-
erty , or against the owner with respect thereto , while such prop-
erty is in the original package , or as fungible goods in bulk in
such warehouse .
Signed March 21 , 1964

parental custody or control , then upon the child itself . If the
writ is served upon the child, the board shall place the child in
the temporary custody of the home , or of some private person
or public agency until the final order of the court . Notice of the
time of the hearing shall be served on the parents or the person
having actual custody or control of the child, and such person
shall have the right to call witnesses and be heard as to his rights ,
fitness and ability to care for and educate the child .
(2) If the facts set forth in the petition are found to be
true and any of the conditions set forth in KRS 201.090 exist , the
court shall order that the child be committed to the custody of
the board . If the parents are able to maintain and support the
child, the court shall require them to pay board , and may enforce
such payment by attachment or other proceedings as in cases of
contempt .
(3) An appeal may be taken from any orders , judgments
or commitments made under the provisions of this section ac-
cording to the procedures established in KRS 208.380 for appeals
from the juvenile session of county court .

CHAPTER 177
(Н. В. 442
)
AN ACT relating
tothe compensation
ofwitnesses
.
Be
itenacted
bythe General Assembly
ofthe Commonwealth
ofKentucky
:

642 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 1. A new section of Chapter 421 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
( 1) Each circuit clerk shall pay to each witness the compen-
sation allowed to him by law, under appropriations provided by
the General Assembly and in accordance with procedures govern-
ing expenditures from appropriations . He shall take a receipt for
each payment made by him showing the date on which payments
is made . For the purpose of providing for such payments , the
State Treasurer , on direction of the Department of Finance , shall
advance to each circuit clerk an imprest fund sufficient for the
payment of the fees of all Commonwealth's witnesses for a term
of court or , if the court is one of continuous session , sufficient for
a period of time not to exceed sixty days .
( 2 ) The circuit clerk shall be allowed an amount equal to
five percent of the funds disbursed by him to witnesses under
this section .
Section 2. Subsection ( 1) of Section 421.020 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Witnesses subpoenaed for the state in felony cases shall be
allowed [out of the State Treasury ] one dollar per day for each
day in attendance in the circuit court , beginning with the day
set for the trial of the case . If a witness resides in another county
he shall receive in addition to the per diem , four cents per mile
going and returning by nearest traveled route to be allowed by
the circuit court and certified to the circuit clerk [Department
of Finance ] for payment .
Section 3. Section 421.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
A Commonwealth's witness in a felony case who resides in
another state shall be allowed his necessary expenses in addition
to the per diem , but no such allowance shall be made unless the
judge of the court has made an order based upon the personal
knowledge of the circuit judge or upon information showing that
the testimony of the witness is material to the state , requiring
the attendance of the witness . The expense and per diem shall
be allowed by the circuit court and certified to the circuit clerk
[Department of Finance ] for payment .

CHAPTER 178 643
Section 4. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 28.190 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Each circuit clerk shall be liable on his official bond to
account for the full amounts [ amount ] advanced to him under
KRS 28.180 and Section ( 1) of this Act. Each circuit clerk shall
file a separate statement concerning each imprest fund with the
Department of Finance , in such form as the department pre-
scribes , showing the amounts disbursed since the last preceding
report , accompanied by a receipt for each amount disbursed
signed by the person to whom it was paid, and showing the
date of the payment [ and the days of service for which payment
was made , ] and such other information as the department pre-
scribes . These reports [ This report ] shall be forwarded to the
Department of Finance within thirty [twenty ] days after the
term of court has ended, or in the case of courts of continuous
session , within thirty [ twenty ) days after the end of the period
for which funds are advanced .
Section 5. This Act shall become effective on July 1 , 1964 .
Signed March 21 , 1964
CHAPTER 178
(Н. В. 446)
AN ACT prohibiting collusion among bidders on state invitations and
false swearing in regard thereto and providing penalties therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of Chapter 42 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
(1) Any agreement or collusion among bidders or prospec-
tive bidders which restrains , tends to restrain , or is reasonably
calculated to restrain competition by agreement to bid at a fixed
price , or to refrain from bidding , or otherwise , is prohibited .
(2 ) Each bid submitted on state invitations shall be ac-
companied by a sworn statement that the bidder has not been

644 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
a party to any agreement or collusion to restrain or otherwise
limit competition .
Section 2. A new subsection of Section 42.990 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
(1) Any person who violates any of the provisions of sub-
section 1 of Section 1 of this Act shall , upon conviction , be fined
not less than five thousand dollars nor more than ten thousand
dollars , or be imprisoned not less than one year nor more than
five years , or both such fine and imprisonment . Any firm, corpora-
tion , or association which violates any of the provisions of sub-
section 1 of Section 1 of this Act shall , upon conviction , be fined
not less than ten thousand dollars nor more than twenty thousand
dollars .
( 2 ) Any person who violates the provisions of subsection
2 of Section 1 of this Act shall be subject to prosecution under
KRS 432.170 .
Signed March 21 , 1964

CHAPTER 181
(Н. В. 451)
AN ACT relating to the powers of cities of the third, fourth , fifth and
sixth classes to condemn property for sewerage systems and sewage
treatment plants .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 94.170 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is hereby amended to read as follows :
Whenever any city of the third, fourth , fifth or sixth class
determines to acquire or construct a sewerage system under the
provisions of KRS 94.160 , and the city cannot agree with the
owner as to the compensation for the sale of any property located
either within or without the boundaries of the city that is neces-
sary to complete the system , the city may, after adopting and
publishing an ordinance declaring the necessity, condemn and
take possession of the property for that purpose in the manner
provided in KRS 416.010 to 416.080 .
Section 2. Whereas cities of the Third Class were inad-
vertently omitted from the provisions of this section and in part
now face the necessity of availing themselves of the provisions
hereof , an emergency is declared to exist and this act shall become
effective upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
.

CHAPTER 182
(Н. В. 452
)
AN ACT relating to the placement of children for adoption
.
Be
itenacted by the General Assembly
ofthe Commonwealth
ofKentucky
:
Section
1. Section 199.470
ofthe Kentucky Revised
Statutes
isamended to read as follows
:
(1) Any adult person who
is
aresident
ofthis state
or
who has resided
inthis state for twelve months next before filing
,

CHAPTER 182 647
may file a petition for leave to adopt a child in the circuit court
of the county in which the petitioner resides .
(2) If petitioner is married , the husband or wife must join
in a petition for leave to adopt a child unless the petitioner is
married to a natural parent of the child to be adopted , except
that if the court finds that the requirement of a joint petition
would serve to deny the child a suitable home , such requirement
may be waived .
(3) No petition for the adoption of any child under the age
of sixteen shall be filed until after the child has lived continuously
in the home of the petitioner for at least three months imme-
diately prior to the filing of the petition .
(4) No petition for adoption shall be filed unless prior to
the filing of the petition the child sought to be adopted has been
placed for adoption by a licensed child-placing institution or
agency or by the department , or the child has been placed with
written approval of the commissioner ; but no such approval shall
be necessary in the case of :
( a) A child sought to be adopted by a stepparent , grand-
parent , sister , brother , aunt or uncle ;
(b ) A child received by the proposed adopting parent or
parents from an agency without this state with the written consent
of the commissioner .
( 5) Subsection ( 4) of this section shall not apply to those
children placed for adoption prior to June 14, 1962 .
(6) All persons other than a licensed child- placing agency
or institution , [ and ] the department or persons excepted by
KRS 199.470 (4) or 199.470 (5) who wish to place or receive a
child shall make written application to the commissioner for
permission to place or receive a child . Upon receipt of an appli-
cation to place or receive a child the commissioner shall cause an
investigation to be made of the home and the background of the
person or persons wishing to receive the child . The purpose of the
investigation shall be to determine the suitability of the appli-
cants to receive a child, taking into account at all times the best
interest of the child for whom application to receive has been
made . The child welfare worker making the investigation shall

648 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
make a finding in writing recommending either that the applica-
tion be granted or that the application be denied . In either case
reasons for the child welfare worker's recommendation shall be
given in writing . The recommendation of the child welfare
worker shall then be reviewed by the commissioner . Based on
the report and recommendation of the child welfare worker
making the investigation , the commissioner shall grant or refuse
permission for the applicant to place or receive a child as early
as practicable , but, in any case, the decision shall be made within
60 days after the receipt of the application . In reaching his de-
cision the commissioner shall be guided by the ability of the
persons wishing to receive the child to give the child a suitable
home , and shall at all times consider the best interest of the child
from a financial , medical , psychological and psychiatric stand-
point . If the application is refused the commissioner shall in
general terms furnish in writing the reasons for his refusal .
In any case where the department refuses to approve the
placement of a child for adoption when requested by the parent
or parents of the child, or refuses the request of any person or
persons that a child be placed with him or them for adoption , the
decision of the commissioner [department ] in so refusing shall
be final unless within ten days after notice of refusal , the natural
or proposed adopting parent or parents shall appeal to the cir-
cuit court of the county in which the adoption is proposed , pro-
vided however that no placement shall be disapproved on the
basis of the religious , ethnic , or inter -faith background of the
adoptive applicant , if such placement is made with the con-
sent of the parent . The department shall be made a party
defendant to the appeal . In the hearing of an appeal the court
shall review the findings of the commissioner and shall determine
if the commissioner has acted arbitrarily , unlawfully , or in such
a manner as to constitute an abuse of discretion .
(7) No person , association or organization , other than the
department or a licensed child-placing institution or agency shall
place a child or act as intermediary in the placement of a child
for adoption or otherwise ; except in the home of a stepparent,
grandparent , sister, brother , aunt or uncle , or upon written
approval of the commissioner . Nothing in this subsection shall
be construed to limit the Department of Economic Security in

CHAPTER 184
(Н. В. 459 )
AN ACT relating to Fire Protection Districts and to Volunteer fire
department districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 75.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Upon a petition signed by at least fifty - one percent
of the voters, as determined by the registration books covering
such territory , and living within the boundary of a proposed fire
protection district or volunteer fire department district filed in
the circuit court clerk's office of the county in which the greater
part of such territory is located , not less than twenty days before
the commencement of the next regular term of said court , and
in courts of continuous sessions not less than twenty days before
the first day of the next calendar month , notice of the filing of
such petition and the object thereof having been published in
two issues of some newspaper of general circulation in the county ,
and by notices posted for at least ten days before the commence-
ment of the said court , one at the courthouse door , and three
others at public places within the bounds of the proposed fire
protection district or volunteer fire department district , the peti-
tion setting out the metes and bounds of the proposed fire protec-
tion district or voluteer fire department district , the number
of voters thereof , and such other facts as may be thought proper ,
on the second day of the term , and in courts of continuous session
on the day provided for in such notice , the court shall , if the
proper notice has been given , and publication made , and no

CHAPTER 184 651
:
:
written objection , remonstrance or defense is interposed , enter
a judgment establishing a fire protection district or a volunteer
fire department district in accordance with the prayer of the
petition , naming it, and describing the boundary thereof by metes
and bounds . Defense , objection or remonstrance may be made
to the petition by any inhabitant of the proposed fire protection
district or a volunteer fire department district , or any person
owning property therein , and if defense, objection or remon-
strance is made, the court shall hear and determine the same , and
render a judgment establishing or refusing to establish the fire
prevention district or volunteer fire department district as may
seem proper . The pleadings and practice as herein provided shall
be the same as in equity cases , as nearly as may be practicable ,
with right of appeal to the Court of Appeals in accordance with
the Rules of Civil Procedure . Such appeal shall be advanced upon
the docket .

percent or more of the freeholders of the territory sought to be
annexed or stricken off may , at any time before the next regular
term of said court , and in courts of continuous session at any time
before the date fixed in the notice , remonstrate in writing , filed in
the clerk's office , to the action proposed . If such written re-
monstrance be filed said clerk shall promptly give notice to the
trustees of the fire protection district , or of the volunteer fire
department district , and the court shall promptly set the case
for a hearing . If upon such hearing , the court finds from the evi-
dence that a failure to annex or strike off such territory will
materially retard the functioning of the fire protection district
or the volunteer fire department district and materially affect
adversely the owners and the inhabitants of the territory sought
to be annexed or stricken off, it shall enter a judgment , granting
the annexation or striking off the territory . In the latter event ,
no new petition to annex or strike off all or any part of the same
territory shall be entertained for a period of two years . The
pleadings and practice herein provided shall be the same as
in equity cases , as nearly as may be practicable , with right of
appeal to the Court of Appeals in accordance with the Rules of
Civil Procedure . Such appeal shall be advanced upon the
docket .
( 2 ) The property in any territory annexed to a fire protec-

be liable to taxation for the purpose of paying any indebtedness
incurred by the fire protection district or the volunteer fire

territory , except such indebtedness as represents balance owing
on purchase price of fire fighting equipment . The property in any
territory stricken off from a fire protection district or a volunteer
fire department district by the incorporation of or annexation
by a city of this Commonwealth shall not be relieved of liability

CHAPTER 187
(Н. В. 472)
AN ACT relating to assistant Commonwealth's attorneys .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
In counties containing a population of 40,000 or more per-
sons but not containing a city of the second class , the Common-
wealth's attorney may, if the fiscal court concurs therein , appoint
an assistant Commonwealth's attorney having a term of four years
subject to removal at any time by the Commonwealth's attorney.
Such assistant Commonwealth's attorney may receive an annual
salary , payable in equal monthly installments out of the county
levy , the annual salary to be set by the fiscal court , within the
limits and in accordance with KRS 64.530 , at not less than
$4,000.00 per year nor more than $6,000.00 per year .
Signed March 21 , 1964

on November 2, 1965. The fact of such submission shall be pub-

(1) It shall be the duty of the Attorney General to prepare
and furnish to the Secretary of State , not later than September
14 , 1965 , a form of "Notice of Election ," which shall be so desig-

law for notices of elections , and further shall set forth the proper
ballot question in full , and provisions of this Act in full , with pro-

of such form of "Notice of Election " to each of five daily news-
papers of bona fide general circulation , published in different
areas of the Commonwealth and selected in the discretion of the
Secretary of State, with directions that the same be published in
each of such newspapers in the form of a legal notice , in one issue

(3) Each county court clerk shall cause a facsimile of the

(4) If the Secretary of State shall perform in proper and

Section 6. Said election shall be conducted in conjunction
with the general election otherwise provided by law to be held
on the same day , by the same election officers and at the same
polling places . The votes cast in favor of and against this Act
shall be canvassed and certified to the State Board of Election

votes cast on the question were in favor of this Act the State
Board of Election Commissioners shall so certify to the Governor
and the Governor shall proclaim that this Act has been approved
and is in effect .

CHAPTER 189 673
CHAPTER 189
(Н. В. 475 )
AN ACT relating to absentee voting .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 126.140 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
As used in this chapter , unless the context otherwise requires :
(1 ) "Absent voter " means any qualified voter [in the
United States services , or bona fide , full time student in residence
at an educational institution ] who expects to be absent from the
county in which is situated his voting precinct on the day of
holding any election .
( 2 ) "Election ” means any regular election , primary election
or special election , for any federal , state , district , county or city
office , including school district offices , and any election upon a
public question .
( 3 ) "Mail " means the delivery to a mail carrier or the
deposit in a letter box or post office of a letter , with postage
prepaid.
(4) For the purpose of absentee registration , " United
States services " means :
( a) Members of the armed forces while in the active
service , and their spouses and dependents residing with them .
(b) Members of the merchant marine of the United States ,

Section 7. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Beginning on the nineteenth day prior to the election during
the hours in which the county court clerk's office is open , the
county board of election commissioners and the county clerk ,
together with interested candidates or their representatives , and
representatives of news media , shall examine all applications for
an absent voter's ballot for the purpose of determining the voter's
right to make application , as well as the validity of the form and
method of submitting the application itself . The county clerk
shall have the registration records at the meeting . The county
clerk shall read the name of each applicant aloud , and the
applicant and his application may be challenged by anyone
present . If the applicant or his application is challenged , the
board shall check the applicant's signature with the signature on
the registration record . The board shall determine whether or
not each challenged applicant shall be accepted or rejected .
If the application is rejected it shall be so marked and the voter
notified of this fact and the reason therefor . No ballot shall be
sent in response to a rejected application or prior to the examina-
tion provided for in this section .
( 2 ) The provisions of subsection ( 1 ) of this section shall
not apply to applications submitted by members of the United
States services .

or post card used by any member of the United States services
for inspection and examination ,

CHAPTER 190
(Н. В. 479 )
AN ACT relating to amphetamine drugs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 217.720 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 217.720 to 217.790 and in this Act unless the
context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Amphetamine " means any drug consisting in whole
or in part of racemic amphetamine sulphate , dextro amphetamine
sulphate , mephentermine , methamphetamine , or any other drug
designated by regulation of the State Board of Health and State
Board of Pharmacy , or any salt of any of these compounds [ ;
except preparations for use in the nose and unfit for internal use ] ;
( 2 ) "Practitioner " means a person licensed under the pro-
fessional licensing laws of this state to prescribe and administer
medicines and drugs ;
( 3 ) "Pharmacist " means a person licensed as a pharmacist
under the pharmacy licensing law of this state .
Section 2. Section 217.730 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby amended to read as follows :
(1) It shall be unlawful for any person to possess , manufac-
ture, sell , dispense, prescribe or administer any amphetamine
except as authorized in KRS 217,720 to 217.790 [ . ] or this Act .

CHAPTER 190 683
(2) (a) No person shall obtain or attempt to obtain an
amphetamine , or procure or attempt to procure the administration
of an amphetamine by fraud , deceit , misrepresentation , or subter-
fuge, or by the forgery or alteration of a prescription , or by the
concealment of a material fact , or by the use of a false name or
the giving of a false address .
(b) Information communicated to a practitioner in an effort
unlawfully to procure an amphetamine , or unlawfully to procure
the administration of such drug , shall not be deemed a privileged
communication .
(c) No person shall willfully make a false statement in or
regarding any prescription , order , report , or record required by
KRS 217.720 to 217.790 or this Act .
(d) No person shall , for the purpose of obtaining an am-
phetamine , falsely assume the title of or represent himself to be
a manufacturer , wholesaler , pharmacist , practitioner , or other
authorized person .
(e) No person shall make or utter any false or forged pre-
scription .
(f) No person shall affix any false or forged label to a pack-
age or receptacle containing any amphetamine .
(3) No person shall bring into , or cause to be brought into ,
or possess , use , or sell, upon the premises of any penal institution
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky , any amphetamine in
violation of KRS 217.720 to 217.790 or this Act .
Section 3. Section 217.994 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby amended to read as follows :
(1) Any person who violates any provision of subsection
(2) or (3) of KRS 217.730 shall be guilty of a felony and shall be
punished by confinement in the penitentiary for not less than
two years nor more than five years , or by a fine of not less than
one thousand dollars nor more than five thousand dollars or both .
(2) Any person who violates any provision of this Act or
KRS 217.720 to 217.790 , for which a specific penalty is not other-
wise provided , shall be punished by confinement in the county
jail for one year or by a fine of not less than three hundred dollars
nor more than five hundred dollars or both .

684 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
[Any person who possesses , manufactures , sells , dispenses ,
prescribes or administers any amphetamine drug , except as
authorized in KRS 217.720 to 217.790 , shall be punished by con-
finement in the county jail for not less than six months nor more
than one year , or by a fine of not less than three hundred dollars
nor more than five hundred dollars , or both , and each violation
shall constitute a separate offense .]
Signed March 21 , 1964

CHAPTER 192
(Н. В. 483)
AN ACT relating to Workmen's Compensation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 342.001 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :

688 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
As used in this Chapter unless the context requires otherwise ;
(1) "Board" means the Workmen's Compensation Board ;
(2) " Director ” means the director of the Workmen's Com-
pensation Board .
Section 2. Section 342.005 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1) This chapter shall apply to all employers having three
or more employes regularly engaged in the same occupation or
business , and to their employes , except that it shall not apply
to domestic employment , agriculture , steam railways , or such
common carriers other than steam railways for which a rule of
liability is provided by the laws of the United States ; provided ,
however , it shall apply to the operators of threshing machines
used in threshing or hulling grain or seeds . It shall affect the
liability of the employers subject thereto to their employes for
a traumatic personal injury sustained by the employe by acci-
dent , and for disability resulting from occupational diseases as
defined in this chapter , arising out of and in the course of his
employment , or for death resulting from such accidental injury
or occupational disease ; provided , however , that "traumatic per-
sonal injury by accident " as herein defined shall not include
diseases except where the disease is the natural and direct result
of a traumatic injury by accident nor shall it include the results
of a pre -existing disease , whether previously disabling or not ,
but shall include injury or death due to inhalation in mines of
noxious gases or smoke , commonly known as "bad air " , and also
shall include injuries or death due to the inhalation of any kind of
gas , and shall also include occupational disease as defined in this
chapter .
( 2 ) The board shall apportion the aggregate extent and
duration of disability among the contributing causes including ,
but not limited to , the following :
( a ) "Traumatic injury by accident " ;
( b ) Pre -existing disease previously disabling ;
( c ) Pre-existing disease not previously disabling but
aroused into disabling reality by the injury or occupational disease .

CHAPTER 192 689
(3) The board shall , in every case , determine whether the
provisions of KRS 342.120 are applicable , and if so the board shall
proceed in accordance with the law and regulations established
for processing claims involving the Special [ Subsequent Claim ]
Fund .
(4) Any employers and employes who are , by the pro-
visions of this section, excepted from the provisions of this chap-
ter, including employers having less than three employes, may
subject themselves thereto by joint voluntary application to the
board , in writing, for such period as may be stated in the applica-
tion, which shall be irrevocable during such period and effective
thereafter until a written revocation be filed with the board or
the employment be terminated .
(5) All employes of departments , administrative bodies ,
and agencies of the state shall be covered by the provisions of
this chapter . The Commissioner of Finance shall exercise the
elections necessary to extend coverage to those state employes
for which coverage is not otherwise required by this chapter .
