Section 6. Section 342.080 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Compensation to any dependent shall cease at the death
or legal or common -law marriage of such dependent or, in the
case of a child or children, upon attaining the age of twenty years
unless incapacitated from wage earning [ . ] , except that , if the
child or children is enrolled as a full-time student in any ac-
credited educational institution , compensation shall cease upon
the termination of such enrollment or upon attaining the age of
twenty -five years , whichever shall first occur . Upon the cessation
of compensation to or on account of any person the compensation
of the remaining persons entitled to compensation shall , for the
unexpired period during which their compensation is payable , be
that which such persons would have received during such unex-
pired period if they had been the only persons entitled to com-
pensation at the time of the accident .
Section 7. Section 342.095 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) When the injury or occupational disease causes total
disability for work, the employer , during such disability , except
for the first seven days thereof , shall pay the employe a weekly

(3) [ ( 4) ] Compensation for an injury or disability to a

(4) [ (2) ] In case of the following injuries or disabilities
from occupational disease the disability shall be considered total
and permanent :
( a) The total permanent loss of sight in both eyes ;
(b) The loss of both feet at or above the ankle ;
(c ) The loss of both hands at or above the wrist ;
(d) A similar loss of one hand and one foot ;
( e) A similar loss of one hand and one eye ;
( f ) A similar loss of one foot and one eye ;
( g ) A disability to the spine , resulting in permanent and

CHAPTER 192 703
(a) If at the time of the injury which resulted in death or
disability or the last date of injurious exposure preceding death
or disability from an occupational disease :
1. The wages were fixed by the week , the amount so fixed
shall be the average weekly wage;
2. The wages were fixed by the month , the average weekly
wage shall be the monthly wage so fixed multiplied by twelve and
divided by fifty-two ;
3. The wages were fixed by the year, the average weekly
wage shall be the yearly wage so fixed divided by fifty - two ;
4. The wages were fixed by the day , hour , or by the out-
put of the employe, the average weekly wage shall be the wage
most favorable to the employe computed by dividing by thirteen
the wages (not including overtime or premium pay) of said em-
ploye earned in the employ of the employer in the first, second ,
third, or fourth period of thirteen consecutive calendar weeks in
the fifty - two weeks immediately preceding the injury .
5. The employe had been in the employ of the employer
less than thirteen calendar weeks immediately preceding the
injury , his average weekly wage shall be computed under para-
graph (4), taking the wages (not including overtime or premium
pay ) for such purpose to be the amount he would have earned had
he been so employed by the employer the full thirteen calendar
weeks immediately preceding the injury and had worked , when
work was available to other employes in a similar occupation .
6. The hourly wage has not been fixed or can not be as-
certained , the wage for the purpose of calculating compensation
shall be taken to be the usual wage for similar services where
such services are rendered by paid employes .
(b) In occupations which are exclusively seasonal and there-
fore cannot be carried on throughout the year, the average weekly
wage shall be taken to be one - fiftieth of the total wages which
the employe has earned from all occupations during the twelve
calendar months immediately preceding the injury .
(c) In the case of volunteer firemen , police , and civil de-
fense members or trainees , the income benefits shall be based on
the average weekly wage in their regular employment .

disability , that fact may be considered in computing his average
weekly wage .
( e) When the employe is working under concurrent con-
tracts with two or more employers and the defendant employer
has knowledge of such employment prior to the injury , his wages
from all such employers shall be considered as if earned from the
employer liable for compensation.
(f) The term " wages ” as used in this section means , in addi-
tion to money payments for services rendered , the reasonable
value of board , rent , housing , lodging , and fuel or similar ad-
vantage received from the employer , and gratuities received in
the course of employment from others than the employer to the
extent such gratuities are reported for income tax purposes .
(g) The board shall , from time to time , based upon the
best available information , determine by regulation industries
which ordinarily do not have a full working day for five days
in every week . In such industries compensation shall be com-
puted at the average weekly wage earned by the employe at the
time of injury reckoning wages as earned while working full-

for five working days in every week regardless of whether the

Section 15. Section 342.215 of the Kentucky Revised

five members appointed by the Governor and shall be attached

services .

for four years and until his successor is appointed and qualified .

circuit judges . The Governor shall designate a member of the
board to serve as chairman for a term of four years . Any vacancy
in the chairmanship shall be filled by the Governor .

Section 19. Section 342.270 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) If the parties fail to reach an agreement in regard to
compensation under this chapter , [or if they have previously filed
such an agreement with the board and compensation has been
paid or is due in accordance therewith and the parties there-
after disagree , ] either party may make written application to the
board for a hearing in regard to the matter at issue and for a
ruling thereon . Such application must be filed within one year
after the accident , or, in case of death , within one year after
such death , or within one year after the cessation of voluntary
payments , if any have been made .
(2) As soon as possible after the application has been re-
ceived the board shall set the date for a hearing , to be held as
soon as is practicable in view of the matter involved , and shall
notify the parties at issue of the time and place of such hearing.
(3 ) Unless otherwise agreed to by the parties and author-
ized by the board , the hearing shall be held at or convenient
to the place where the injury was sustained or the ground for
disagreement occurred . Before directing a hearing , the board ,
[or ] a member thereof [or ] the Director or a hearing officer
[referee ] authorized by the board , may confer informally with
the parties at issue in an attempt to assist in adjusting their dif-
ferences , but may not delay the granting of a hearing , over the
objection of either party , for such purpose .
(4) If the parties have previously filed an agreement which
has been approved by the board and compensation has been
paid or is due in accordance therewith and the parties thereafter
disagree either party may invoke the provisions of KRS 342.125
which remedy shall be exclusive .

compensation are being made under any benefit sections of this
Act to the maximum which the employe's wages shall entitle him ,
unless it shall be shown that the prosecution of the employe's
claim would be prejudiced by delay .

CHAPTER 199
(S. R. 25 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing an appropriation to Betty Sue
Hibbitts , administratrix of the estate of Bessie R. Arnold to reim-
burse the estate for state checks negotiated to the deceased .
WHEREAS , several state checks were negotiated to Bessie R. Arnold
during a period of years running from 1955 to 1960 and which checks
she failed to cash ; and
WHEREAS , state checks are no longer payable two years after the date
on which they were issued; and
WHEREAS , Bessie R. Arnold is now deceased and Betty Sue Hibbitts
has been appointed administratrix of her estate .

728 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. That the estate of Bessie R. Arnold has a valid
claim against the Commonwealth for the aggregate amount of
checks negotiated to her between 1955 to 1960 , which amount is
four hundred and eighty -nine dollars and seventy-eight cents .
Section 2. That the Commissioner of Finance is hereby
authorized and directed to draw a warrant in favor of Betty
Sue Hibbitts , Administratrix in the amount of four hundred and
eighty -nine dollars and seventy-eight cents and transmit the war-
rant to the State Treasurer , who shall issue a check in favor
of Betty Sue Hibbitts , Administratrix in the above amount .
Signed March 21 , 1964
CHAPTER 200
(Н. В. 508 )
AN ACT relating to entertainment booking agents .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Definitions : For purposes of this Act ,
( a) “Entertainment " means any and
alltypes
ofmusical
and acting abilities
, services and performances whether made
,
given
,
ordone
by
asingle person
or
by
agroup
,consisting
oftwo
ormore persons
. Any person engaging
in
orgiving such types
ofmusical
oracting performances shall
beknown
as
anenter-
tainer
, and shall include all singers
, dancers
, actors
, actresses
,
and musical
-instrument players
, who perform for pay
.
(b)
"Booking agent
"means any person
,partnership
orcor-
poration located
orresiding
inthis Commonwealth
orlocated
orresiding
inany other state
ofthe United States outside
of
this Commonwealth
, who contracts for the services
, including

CHAPTER 200 729
scheduling such services as to specific date, time , duration , and
location , of any entertainer as defined in subsection ( a) .
( c ) "Department " means the Department of Revenue .
Section 2. No person shall engage in the occupation of en-
tertainment booking agent until he has paid to the Department
of Revenue the fees prescribed in this section and obtained a
license therefor . Before booking entertainment in this state , each
person so engaged shall pay the following fees for the calendar
year or fraction thereof :
( a) An annual license fee of thirty -five dollars shall accom-
pany each application for a license or the renewal of a license, to
be known as a booking agent's license .
( b ) A performance bond for the amount of five thousand
dollars shall accompany each license application and shall be
posted with the Department of Revenue to insure the perform-
ance of entertainers whose services may be contracted for
through such booking agent .
(c) Any person operating as a booking agent or perform-
ing such services in this state without first having paid the fee
and obtained the license prescribed in this section shall be sub-
ject to a penalty of fifty percent of the amount of the license fee ,
in addition to the fee itself , and six percent interest until paid.
Additionally , any person operating as an entertainment booking
agent or performing such services in this state who willfully fails ,
neglects or refuses to make a report , or to pay the license fees
as herein prescribed , or who shall refuse to permit the depart-
ment to examine the books, papers and records of any promotion
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor, and shall be fined not less than
one hundred dollars nor more than one thousand dollars , or im-
prisoned for a term not to exceed six months , or both .
(d) Any booking agent shall pay to the adversely affected
party an indemnity to be specified by contract for any entertain-
ment not performed as specified by such contract . Failure of the
booking agent to pay such indemnity within sixty days shall be
cause for the Department of Revenue to declare as forfeit all or
that portion of the performance bond necessary to make such
indemnification .
Became law without signature April 1, 1964
